lines.x: Raise that lamp a little higher, I need the light on every detail of the exhibits.
Yes, Curator Hanimefendi!
Who put this statue of a songstress with the sculptures from the Old Calendar Age of Shadows? Completely wrong era. Take it out and move it to Hall Two.
I'm on it, Curator Hanimefendi!
Where is that instrument restored with tech from the Old Calendar Age of Chants? Why don't I see it?
Not here...
Not here either...
♪O guests from afar, ripples in the lotus pond follow thee here♪
♪Neath the tree's shade, sup sweet dates and fresh beer♪
♪Let us toast now to a midsummer, and cheers!♪
Oh, Medjehtiqedti Hanimefendi, your singing rivals even the most elegant of fowlbeasts! I can't tear myself away!
Ah, Medjehtiqedti Hanimefendi, the notes of your lute flow like purest water! Like the calmest, sweetest brook!
But of course. I have worked tirelessly for months to produce the perfect rendition of this ancient ballad.
Hmph. Words sweet as honey, fingers idle as a slumberfoot. No wonder I couldn't find you.
You do know that restored lute is going on display tomorrow, right, Titi?
Do allow the welcoming committee to borrow it for today, Anat. We have an honored guest coming, and the museum must receive her well.
It's a special exhibit for the Flood Festival, Titi.
Our top priority is ensuring there are no errors in any of the sections.
Our visitors can be very fussy about the details. You've been here long enough, you should know better than I.
Let's not get mad now, Curator Hanimefendi.
This is your first major exhibit since taking up your post, so I understand that you're a little anxious about it.
But trust me, Anat, you need to calm down. No matter what you're putting on display, someone is going to nitpick it.
This is Menat-Ha'mait. Every family's got some treasure stashed away somewhere. It's no surprise they'd turn their noses up at our exhibits.
I'm going to check over the new displays for tomorrow.
Don't worry, I won't disturb your date with that esteemed gentleman.
It's an object I'm studying, not my date.
You look at it ever so lovingly, though.
Strange...
Has no-one picked her up yet since we've been talking?
I'm guessing that girl over there is learning how the irrigation channels work, gonna write a thesis on the history of it.
Or maybe she's an epigraphist, and she's just cleaning the sludge off the stele in the river.
What's the difference? They're just different types of history nerd.
Blame it on the city's long history. Every historian in Sargon gets obsessed with this place.
Yeah. Look at this ancient riverway we've got here, four centuries old, broad and wide. And our buildings? A youthful two centuries, yet tall and impressive.
You wanna talk about 'rich historical tradition'? Well, here you have it.
Can any of them beat the Flood Festival, though? We've been celebrating it for over a thousand years.
Oh, when it floods, I'm going to offer up some white oasis flowers to the river god. May His breath be forever filled with their fragrant aroma.
What offering have you prepared, friend?
A cup and plate of pure gold, along with a jar of wine, in accordance with my family traditions. The wine was sealed by my grandfather when my father was born, an excellent vintage.
May He enjoy its taste, and share in the festival spirit with us.
May we meet Him wandering the realm, and earn His blessing.
O immortal Menat-Ha'mait, may He forever grace this city.
Did you find it yet, hanimefendi?
Give me a sec, kiddo, I think I felt it just now.
But you've been standing in the river for half an hour already. You keep saying you almost got it...
Shh! I'm trying to concentrate here...
Seriously, where has our little shahzada run off to now?
I've got people at every gate waiting, but none of them have seen her.
I've put so much thought into welcoming you, how could I let you just slip into the city like this?
Masaa al-kheir, Medjehtiqedti Hanimefendi. Wherever are you going in such a hurry?
Ah, Bekti Hanimefendi, it's you. I'm meeting an old friend of mine!
Here, some oranges I just plucked from my garden. They ripened just before the festival, I'm sure everyone will love them.
Take a few for your friend, Medjehtiqedti Hanimefendi.
She's not a fan of favors, but I thank you in her stead. Salaam, Bekti Hanimefendi.
Ma'a s-salaama!
The people here are so friendly.
*sigh* Let me have a think about where she'd go...
Where did she like to run off back when we were in school?
Ah, I'm really sorry, hanim! I didn't see you there. Forgive me for knocking your oranges on the ground.
I'm fine, how about you? Are you alright?

Thank you for your kindness, hanim.
Oh no...
What's with the frown all of a sudden? Are you hurt?
A shadow flitted across your forehead just now, an ill omen. I fear you will meet with some misfortune soon.
If you are willing to part with five dinars, I can ward it off for you.
Umm... Well, uh, sorry, but I've got an appointment, gotta go now! Bye!
Wait! Two dinars! Just two dinars, then!
This isn't a trick! You really are about to run into some bad luck!
You wouldn't trick me, would you...?
Of course not. I always keep my word.
Really? *sniff* But...
Aw, don't cry, I'll—
Agh! It slipped away again!
Waaaaaahhh!
Ah, no, don't cry! I was just teasing you! I got it! See? It's right here in my hand!
You're a meanie...
*sigh* I'm too used to teasing my little brother back home.
I feel sorry for your poor little brother...
Save your pity, that kid gives me endless headaches. Alright, pass me the jar. I'll put the fin in it for you.
I thought I'd never see my goldfin again... Thanks, hanimefendi.
Tsk, tsk, tsk. I worked my butt off helping you get your finny friend back. Put a little more pep into that 'thanks'.
THANK YOU SO MUCH! YOU'RE THE BEST!
Now that's more like it.
Alright, you'd best hurry home already. No more fancy ideas about trying to take your pet for a walk.
See you, hanimefendi.
Taking a pet fin for a walk in the river? Reminds me of the crazy ideas I had as a kid.
*cough*
Well then. Seems our audience has finally had enough of me groping around in the water for finbeasts.
Glad you still know me so well, Pepe.
*sigh* I really don't want to head to the museum so soon. When Anat's in question-asking mode, I won't even get a moment's rest.
Where is she, anyway? She didn't come with you?
She's acting curator now. Got a lot of things to do. With the upcoming super-important exhibition, she's been busier than ever.
Nice. Never thought she'd get promoted right after she arrived. I remember back in school, nobody thought she'd go far with that meek personality, but I knew she was a hard worker. She'll go far.
She's always been one tough reed. She never yields.
Sure you aren't talking about yourself? You've been in this city for a while. No big changes?
Me? I'm not too busy, but I'm also not too free. I was just barely able to secure the title of vice curator.
Weren't you in charge of stuff at that Columbian museum?
I was in charge of their business operations. Things are different here, you know.
The Menat-Ha'mait museum doesn't need to profit. Annual support from the Lords Ameer and the Padishahs is enough. No use for me.
Which is great. I get to take it easy.
But you always kept yourself busy... back in the day.
How about you? You must have gotten a lot of good stuff out of your travels.
More than you can imagine.
Hmm. Sounds like your report's going to be a lot longer than you can imagine as well.
Oh no. It's so hot here. Feel like I've got sunstroke. I'm gonna stay here and cool down in the breeze.
Save it. Do you know how many times I used that excuse to help you ditch class?
You've been sitting there quite a while, hanim, and the sun is rather fierce. Would you like something to drink? We have our specialty liquorice juice, freshly made. Only five dinars.
Or how about an apple and roselle tea? Two dinars a cup.
No.
Perhaps a glass of apricot juice then? Only one dinar.
I said no.
Very well then...
Hold on, waiter, not so fast. A pot of your best coffee, if you will, and add some local cardamom and cloves.
And a glass of liquorice juice for the lady.
Right away.
Coming to Menat-Ha'mait for the Flood Festival, anyone should have filled their wallet. You won't get much out of your trip like this.
No offense, but I have always been of the belief that man should live within his means. If your funds are insufficient, there are plenty more other places to enjoy a holiday.
I've heard that Dossoles in Bolívar is rather pleasant. Cheap, with beautiful scenery, suitable for those of more... frugal means.
You speak wisdom. A truly educated man you must be.
You flatter me. And yourself? Broadening your horizons on the road?
On the road...? I suppose so. I seek my own road to follow.
Your coffee, effendi.
Here, please.
Your liquorice juice, hanim.
(Drinks it up in a single gulp)
I thank you for your hospitality.

(Quietly) Hmph.
Waiter, could you tell me of some particularly fun activities during your Flood Festival—
Why the look?
Forgive me, effendi. I am simply amazed that the hanim was so hesitant to part with her money for a drink, yet gave me a sizable tip...
Ah, it appears my fellow traveler was of small means but great pride.
No matter, I shall find some amusements by myself. The bill please, waiter.
Fifteen dinars in total, effendi.
Effendi?
Err... Wherever has my coin-purse gone?
May the river wash away these gilded scraps of metal. May those who need them find them as its waters rise.
Enough time wasted. I nearly lost sight of my target.
I've heard the City of Radiance is adorned in jewels, and that countless brave souls have sought out its treasures without success.
And so I walked the long road and arrived here, only to find miser after miser bickering over a few gold coins.

A dark cloud floats by, blocking the glaring rays of the sun. The red-maned woman pours coins out of her purse, tossing them into the river with an even hand. The ripples tear apart the sun's reflection.
Then she disappears into the crowd, whistling.
Ahh... The Grand Bazaar's apricot juice still hits the spot. Tastes pretty much the same as it did back when we were in school!
This is where I thought you'd be, but it took me some long, fruitless searching to remember that we would only ever come here the three of us.
When you were alone, you would always go to...
The river. I love strolling along the river by myself. Didn't think you'd run all over the place to find me.
You are an honored guest, Pepe.
We're friends, you don't need to be so particular about all this...
You didn't organize some toe-curlingly cringey performance to welcome me back, did you?
That would be the proper rites and ceremonies.
Save it! I can hear the cogs in your brain grinding away as you cook up your evil machinations.
So what big discovery got you coming back here?
A jewel.
A jewel?

Sapphire bracelets, necklaces and earrings, make a set of your choosing, only two thousand dinars a set! If you like it then buy it, no haggling, no bargaining. Only two thousand dinars and they're yours!
Dear customers, we are now offering a lovely selection of hand-selected emerald rings. Enjoy a discount if you buy an entire box!
Guaranteed quality and weight, while still economical. Every one fifteen carats! Buy some to send home to your family and friends, only four thousand five hundred dinars for the box!
Rubies, sapphires, chrysoberyl, amethysts, rose quartz, olivine! For gems, come to the Grand Bazaar, where we offer bulk trade and low prices, all day every day!
Menat-Ha'mait's drowning in all sorts of jewels. I don't think you're turning heads if it's any regular old gem.
Hey, this jewel's pretty extraordinary, okay?
Extraordinary?
I recently bought a piece of pink kyanite. They say it was once used for purifying water. The Originium circuit carved into it is incredibly delicate, a work of art in its own right.
Sounds rather valuable. I bought a similar tourmaline just last month. The power of my Arts improved substantially after laying it into my cane.
Both cannot compare with this treasure of mine. Look.
Isn't this a limited edition gemstone belt from last year? I remember the peddler said it could rejuvenate your skin and slow your aging.
Do you feel anything wearing it?
I do feel rather more refreshed...
Eh, compared to the ones going around the Grand Bazaar, the one I found can do something really special.
Special how? These Originium-etched jewels being sold here are already pretty special for anyone coming from outside the city.
Let's get walking first, Titi.
Don't tell me there's some kind of dirty secret behind it?
No way! Relax, I'll tell you, but—
Before this scorcher of a sun gets too high in the sky, I want to run by Septagram Avenue to buy an idol. Even research needs the blessing of the gods, right?
You best walk quick then. That road's going to be too hot for your feet real soon.
The target is moving toward the museum in the heart of the city...
Considering she was personally welcomed by the vice-curator, it appears my information was correct. She is no ordinary woman.
What?! The jewel you found was a relic of the Shah?
Man... I shouldn't have worn open-toed sandals. I'm going to have sandal-patterned feet later.
Seriously? You're thinking about your sandals now? Where did you find that jewel?
I got a clue in a place once known as Szubat-Arsalan. After a few twists and turns, I finally found it in the territory of a Lord Ameer.
The Lionpride's Ancient Throne...
I also saw some ancient images left by the Shah himself in a cave full of crystals.
And I wrote everything down in this journal. It'll make public the true location of the great trove, and prove that the Shah's promise to the Nightzmora was more than just a legend.
Just think what might be waiting for us among all those riches?
Yet no matter how valuable that stuff may be, the true treasure is the clues that will help us reconstruct the truth of that era.
We'll find the answers to the Shah's unsolved mysteries one by one, Titi!
Why would he make such a crazy declaration of war? Or what really happened in the legendary clash with Kharanduu Khagan in the south! Or his final resting place!
These discoveries will shock the people! All Shar-Agade, the entire City of Gold, will be looking my way!
If you know where it is, just take a team there to excavate it. It's just—
No, there's one last trail to follow. I need to borrow an artifact from the museum.
You're the daughter of a Padishah. The museum storehouses are open to you, anything you want that isn't on public display.
Thaaaaat's the issue.
Wait. Don't tell me you want...
Pepe looks at the facade of the museum, a banner slowly unfolding across it.
The banner depicts a slender mummy draped in golden armor, its face hidden by a death mask.
And at the end of that banner is written in great, big script:
Landmark Exhibit Commemorating Varjavandabad Museum's Anniversary—The Nameless General of the Shah's Very Court!
The General? No, he is the guardian of the trove.
No matter who it actually is, it's Anat's most prized exhibit. I don't think you're going to borrow it that easily.
Well, a jewel with clues won't prove anything by itself. I can only show my worth to my father and the Shahanshah by finding the Shah's trove.
If I do that, I can go to the City of Gold, become the Shahanshah's Historian of Shar-Agade, and write an account of all Sargonian history.
Being a Padishah's heir not good enough for you, Pepe?
The wealth and authority is just superficial prosperity. The true honor that runs through our family is the role of court historian, passed down from generation to generation.
But... Hasn't your father always wanted your brother to be the court historian?
Well, we both have the same basic qualities. But I'm not losing to him on ability either.
Pepe, the City of Gold's envoy is taking your brother there in two months.
I'll make it in time, Titi! I just need you to help me! Anat's only willing to lend me that mummy to study come autumn.
But this summer is my last opportunity. I can't wait that long.
You and your brother... You were always fighting as kids, and now you're at loggerheads over the same court appointment.
...Here. My pass. Take it.
You can sneak into the exhibition hall at night and study it, quietly.
Titi...
I'm just sick of seeing you two bicker and quarrel these past few years.
This is the last time I'll help you. If in the end, you come up empty-handed, then please don't keep stubbornly trudging on.
I won't. I won't come up empty-handed.
Ignoring Pepe's murmuring, Medjehtiqedti stands up to look at the grand edifice, witness to centuries of history.
The sun shines upon all the gardens and buildings, and under the boundless blue sky, the great trapezoid looms over them.
The leaves of tall palm trees besides them rustle in the wind.
I might see it every day, but I'm still stunned by its grandeur.
For so long has it quietly guarded the city, fending off time itself in silence.
Yep. A human lifespan is nothing compared to it, no matter how long it may be.
Over in the blink of an eye.
If only we could stop time for a moment.
Yeah. That would be great.
See you at the exhibition tomorrow. If you get caught, just say you stole the pass.
The exhibition... tomorrow...
Good. The guest's itinerary has been confirmed. I'll welcome her properly.
A descendant of a glorious clan of historians, the one who spreads ballads of kings and heroes, explorer of all Sargon, purveyor of knowledge, a respected Padishah's daughter—
Djerwnapekhaper Saciut Hatshepsut.
Any other would worry they would forget all these appellations, but the one who stands here is Narantuya, and her only regret is that these titles are all held by one person.
Fine. I guess the ransom ought to settle my debts even if it's just one girl.
The money left in this purse should be enough to buy a ticket for tomorrow...
Never mind.
The line would be too long, and the sun too hot.
And in this city, gold coins are too common to be worth anything.
Do you swear by your golden sun, your splendid gems and the great museum behind you?
That you will answer prudently on account of your respect for history and civilization alike?
Look within, at your own conscience, and tell me once more. How much is a ticket to the museum?
I already told you, hanim. The hall for non-Sargonian artifacts is free of charge. Only the Sargonian exhibition hall needs tickets.
If you don't understand, we have the prices for our tour services here on the window. You can hire one of our translators too, if needed.
So the Minoan exhibition hall is free to enter?!
I'll say it once more, hanim. There is no 'Minoan exhibition hall'.
But at times there will be a few Minoan artifacts on display in the Northeastern Tributary exhibit.
...'Northeastern Tributary'?
The foreign traveler takes a deep breath, the muscles of her bare arm bulging as she clenches her fist.
A part of the ticketer's mind is telling her to pull the booth's shutter closed, but the traveler's other hand is firmly holding it open.

History is a grand river beside which all who live upon this great land trek. The Originium workshops of the Ancient Town of Tal'sas were unearthed by Gaulish explorers. The scrolls detailing the legends of old Agamem were restored by Minoan artisans.
But Minos's own history has gone against the current, and has found itself scattered across the golden sands. I have traced its path just to catch a glimpse of its fragments.
Whether or not you agree, a historian's mission is to find the past that people have forgotten.
For the sake of this duty, should we not prevent arrogant discrimination by history's tyrannical plunderers?
In other words, if you believe that Sargonian history is worth a hundred dinars to see, then should Minoan history not stand equal?
Do you want a ticket or not, hanim?
—Even more importantly, Sargonians, as it so happens, are the plunderers, in this case.
With all that said, are you willing to hold fast to your conscience and confront the true value of Minos's artifacts?
Next!
Wait.
My name is Aspasia ho Herias. Please, use the Minoan spelling, not a Sargonian transliteration.
A ticket, if you would so please.
♪Night blankets the riverbanks♪
♪Twin moons rise in loving embrace♪
♪In the dark they kiss♪
♪Then the stars illuminate♪
♪And they blush in their glow♪
Whoa!
You gave me a fright, girl! What are you doing here in the middle of the night?
Scared I'm gonna rob you?
Of course not. It's only rich folks around here.
Shouldn't a girl your age be on a date down at Riverside Avenue at this hour? What are you doing in front of the museum?
I'm on the night shift.
Ugh, the youth these days! My lamps used to shine on young couples in the throes of passion, but now you're all doing overtime at ungodly hours.
Go on, I've lit your path. Have a safe trip home when you get off.
Thanks for that!
There really is no one here... guess Titi wasn't messing with me.
Let's see if they've picked up anything new.
Gem workshop artifacts from the Age of Shadow. Hmm... The Originium circuits on this one is really something... assembled from multiple pieces?
No, it was crafted out of a single piece of Originium. Amazing work. Really have to look close to see it.
Wow, ritual instruments from the Age of Flow, found in the jungles. Are those blood spots from ritual sacrifices?
And all these weapons... A ceremony from the Battle of Turbah Al-Sawda?
Well, I'll get to know you later. I have an appointment to keep.
It's here...
Nice to meet you, old man! Sorry for disturbing your peace and quiet tonight, but I have questions that only you can answer.
Please don't get mad at me.
Wow, amazing!
Feline, approximately two meters tall. Most of the body has been replaced with mechanical parts, integrated with the remaining biological tissue.
Skin color is purple, likely from the preservative fluids used, and it remains supple to the touch.
A golden mask and a suit of golden armor. All internal organs have been removed.
Strange. Neither the mummy nor the funerary objects give any clue to the location of the trove.
But everything from the site is here.
Perhaps it's the gem itself that offers a hint...
'First, the heart. At its first beat, we come to be.'
'Second, the lungs. At their first breath, we come to feel.'
'Third, the bowels. We fill them with food, cultivate our tongues, and develop our own likings.'
'Fourth, the liver. We go into the night, awake and alert, for duty and obligation.'
Now how do I use this gem?
Pepe ponders for a while, then reaches into the mummy's chest cavity, placing the gem where the heart should be.
She holds her breath, waiting for something to happen.
Nothing?
No change at all...
No images or text appear. Pepe frowns and tries to retrieve the gem.
Perhaps she simply placed it wrong.
But as soon as she reaches into the chest cavity, she senses that something is off.
There should be an empty space, but it feels like she's put her hand into water. The gem is right there before her eyes, but she feels like she's groping in a bottomless pond.
Huh...
Who goes there? Have you any idea what time it is?
Oh no, it's security.
Pepe closes the glass display case lightly, and sneaks into the darkness, holding her breath.
Who's there?
I thought I heard something.
Was it over here?
(Don't come this way, no!)
Hey, is someone there?
(Yikes! He's right behind me...)
Who is it?!
(There's someone else?)
Meow?
Oh, just a little critter. How did you get in here, cutie?
Meow!
Hey, don't run! Look, I have a can of fins in oil!
(I need to get the gem...)
Where did it go? I just turned a corner and it's gone?
(Why are you coming back?!)
Did the last shift forget to shut the windows? Hot air's blowing in.
I guess that explains the critter. Can't let it run around the museum.
Mroaw—!
Mroaaaw—!
I don't care whose pet you are, one more sound from you and I'll have you castrated tomorrow!
(Softly) Meow...?
*yawn* Sorry, Mio, I was fast asleep and didn't hear you outside.
(Shakes tail irritably)
Did you see that one?
(Nods)
Good work, Mio.
We've waited for so long.
Bring him to me as soon as you can.
(Nods)
Come, I have some jerky for you.
(Nudges with affection)
Pepe...
What were you doing last night?
Come now... You have to tell me...
I-It wasn't me! I didn't do it! I stayed in the whole night! Whatever it is, I didn't do it!
Pepe...?
Anat...?
Pepe, did you... sleep last night?
Why do you ask? I feel fine.
You fell asleep in the middle of a conversation...
*yawn*... Morning, ladies.
You reek of alcohol, Titi. Where were you last night?
I took Pepe out on the town. Not like she's in town every day, after all.
How did you even wake up after drinking so much yesterday? And not one whiff of booze on you? Did you shower?
Oh... yeah! My brain was all foggy, so I took a cold shower.
The opening ceremony is today, Titi.
And you took Pepe out drinking??? Don't I have enough to worry about already?!
Hey, I came first thing in the morning. I've looked at all the details of the exhibition. Your arrangements are perfect, except...
Is something not good enough? Just tell me, while we still have a chance to make changes.
You're too high strung today. Settle down.
Just focus on your speech. Leave this to me, and I guarantee your exhibition will go smoothly.
*sigh* Get changed before you make any promise to me. The guests will complain if they smell all that liquor on you.
(Whisper) That look on your face. What trouble did you get yourself into? Explain.
(Whisper) I tried putting that gem into the mummy's chest cavity last night. I was about to retrieve it when a security guard showed up.
(Whisper) Get me a bit of time alone with the mummy once the exhibition is over. I'll take the gem back.
(Whisper) Thankfully, no one has noticed anything wrong. You'll have an hour, at most. If you still can't get it out, I'll have to report that my pass got stolen.
(Whisper) Don't worry, if I can't get it out, I'll apologize to Anat my self.
(Whisper) How did you get that smell onto you anyway?
(Whisper) I poured a bottle of wine on myself before I came in.
Explains why you're all sticky.
How are preparations going over there, Ajani?
No problem, Narantuya. The smoke emitter is set, and we'll have our commotion on your signal.
Your status, Ajazi?
I'm in position.
Good. She's a Padishah's daughter, and there will be big trouble if we don't make this a clean kidnapping.
I don't understand why you insist on doing it here with all these visitors around.
The more people, the better.
When the commotion happens, no one will bat an eye at a few compassionate souls carrying a poor fainted girl out of the room.
They'll be too busy worrying about themselves.
I marked the camera blind spots on your map. Pick the right time to knock her out.
Right, I'll leave early to set an ambush.
Hopefully this needle will give her a good dream. I don't want to have to go into the jungle to make this serum again.
I can't read the hieroglyphs on the sign... excuse me. Do you know how to get to the 'Northeastern Tributary' exhibit?
The Northeastern Tributary exhibit, huh...? I'm not sure. I've never been there.
We could look for it together, if you don't mind. I find the lines of your muscles more beautiful than anything on display here.
Please don't say that. Everything exhibited here is a piece of history.
All these artifacts are worth stopping to admire.
You could have just said you weren't interested and spared me the excuses.
Excuse me. Do you know where the Northeastern Tributary exhibit is?
Let's see... take a right there, walk five hundred meters, then take a left.
Thank you. I've asked quite a few people, but none of them knew where it was.
I understand. The citizens here are proud of Sargon's long history, but they don't care much about faraway places.
You should have seen when the Columbian history exhibit was running. So quiet you could hear a pin drop.
Yes... I can sense the arrogance.
Thank you for showing me the way.
Respect... that's what modern people lack.
Wait, how did I end up in this hall again? I should try the right side this time.
There are more visitors than I expected.
I'm a little worried about Anat. She's fidgeting so much she's going to tear her speech to shreds.
Can't be any worse than her first presentation. It took hours for her to come back to her senses that time.
Hey, where are you going?
Just to have a little chat with her, get her to relax.
Phew...
How are the preparations going, Anat?
I can already recite this speech backwards from memory, but the moment I think about all those eyes fixed on me, listening to every word I say...
What will they think if I stutter, or make a mistake?
They'll all forget by dinner.
The Flood Festival is right around the corner. Who has room to remember one girl's embarrassment during all those festivities?
But I'm nervo—hey, why are you rubbing my ears?
Feel better now?
Yes...
Works every time. I still remember how Pepe and I rubbed your ears like this for an entire day to get you to relax before an interview.
...Thank you, Titi. Very thoughtful.
Bayanlar ve Baylar, take your seats, and welcome Curator Anat El-Aydin onto the stage with a round of warm applause!
Go. It's alright if you make a mistake, just keep going.
If you're so stiff, I'll sneak around behind your back and rub your ears. Better to be embarrassed together than alone, right?
Oh, Titi...
Welcome to the Varjavandabad Museum's special exhibit, commemorating the Flood Festival.
The highlight of the exhibit is an unnamed man, discovered in the Fourth Necropolis Complex on the outskirts of the city, meeting the public for the first time.
Both the body, the armor and the funerary objects buried with him are remarkably well-preserved.
The extraordinary details on his clothing is clear indication of high status.

He has not been identified in any extant Sargonian historical records. Based on the artifacts recovered from his tomb, we theorize that he may have been...
Excuse me, is this the Northeastern Tributary exhibit?
What...?
Did I take a wrong turn?
Yes... the Northeastern Tributary exhibit is on the other side of the museum, near the entrance.
Sorry to interrupt.
...It's fine.
Ahem... in any case... Continuing where we left off...
We have theorized that the mummy is...
Ouch!
Excuse me, sir! Sorry!
Are you blind?!
Honored guests...
My fault! Sorry, sir!
Excuse me, hanimefendi... where are you going?
Urgent business! Please carry on speaking!
But I'm just reaching...
Alright, yes! I know, I'm getting out right now.
...the most important part of the presentation.
Er... where was I...?
Sorry... let me find my place...
Oh, yes... the exhibit behind me...
I-It's...
*yawn*

It's...
Anat spent a long time preparing for this speech.
She prepared for bored audience members to leave, or heckle her for her mistakes.
But she never imagined she would lose her train of thought because an interruption, leaving her speechless.
She tries to collect herself, taking a few deep breaths, forcing a smile toward the crowd. She attempts to continue.
Then she feels a hand on her ear, pressing it gently.
Anat blinks, feeling a sense of warmth rise in her heart.
Thanks, Titi. I'm fine, don't wor—
Anat does not finish the sentence.
She sees Titi looking up at her from the crowd in terror. Pepe's jaw has dropped open so wide that it could fit a whole apple.
If Titi is not behind her, then whose hand is on her ear?
Anat gulps. She dares not turn around.
Eek!
Th-The mummy is moving!
I'm getting out of here! This place is haunted!
It's looking at me!
I knew it!
This is what happens when a city grows too old!
Everyone! Stay calm! Don't panic!
The exit's over here! Stay in line, don't push!
Make way, make way!
Sorry, excuse me.
*Sargon expletive* Anat! Anat!
Medjehtiqedti squeezes through the crowd to find a pale Anat lying limp on the ground, her lips parting weakly as though trying to speak.
But the sight of the mummy cocking its head behind Anat strikes her with such terror that she cannot move her feet.
Titi, go handle the evacuation. I'll try to take Anat and the mummy somewhere else.
Aren't you scared? It's moving!
I'm... alright. Ahem. I've been on two Old Calendar tomb excavations this year and met with their residents personally.
This one looks like a nice old man by comparison.

Don't worry, it takes plenty of strength to do archaeology too.
Titi...
Anat?
She's fine... just muttering in her sleep.
The curator's office isn't far. Go out the side door and avoid the crowds. I'll come find you once the evacuation is complete.
What's going on? I'm not in my ambush position yet! Did you turn on the smoke early?
We didn't do anything.
What's happening?
Seems like someone else is causing a commotion.
Drat... I didn't see the target in the crowd. Where did she go?
Alright, change of plans. We're starting early. Find her, keep an eye on her, and keep me posted.
Roger.
Hurry. We need to blend into the crowd once we're done.
It'll be too late once the evacuation is finished.
Good... everyone is gone...
Pepe approaches the mummy and sees a blue light glowing in its chest.
The gem has vanished into the light and is nowhere to be seen.
Hrmph. So it was the gem...
It's not easy to carry you like this...
After observing the mummy for a few seconds, Pepe decides to push the mummy's raised left arm back down.
(Pushes the left arm down)
(Raises right arm)
Huh?
(Pushes the right arm down)
(Raises left arm)
What's going on with you?
Pepe cocks her head and ponders how to get the mummy to cooperate.
To her surprise, she finds that the mummy also cocks its head, as if in thought.
Wait... are you copying me?
Pepe takes a step to the left, and the mummy does the same.
Hm?
Pepe jumps backwards, and the mummy does the same, making a loud noise as it hits the wall behind it.
Pfft.
Looks like you can move your arms and legs, but not your brains.
Hey, if we can move in unison...
An idea comes to Pepe as she looks at Anat, who still lies passed out on the ground.
Almost to the curator's office. Alright, follow my movements and make a turn here.
(Turns right)
Gotta get it to hold Anat's shoulder tighter. She almost fell just now. Let's see...
(Moves right hand)
(Grabs Anat's shoulder)
(Frowns unconsciously)
Alright. Now follow me...
(Moans unconsciously)
Maybe we should take smaller steps...

I'll consider that a yes.
Very good...
Wait, no, no, no—!
The mummy steps on the hem of its own robe and falls forward, right on top of Anat's tiny frame.
(Groans unconsciously)
That red mark on her face isn't going away for a while...
Oh, you genius.
Come on, hold her tightly this time.
(Frowns unconsciously)
Walk with me. Not too fast, not too slow. Good... one-two... one-two...
Take it slow and easy. We're almost there.
Pepe turns her head to find her pass lying on the floor. It must have fallen out of her pocket.
She looks at her own raised arms, then at the mummy's holding Anat.
If the mummy let go, it would drop Anat on her head...
How do I get into the office like this?
I can't use my hands... I have to use my foot...
(Reaches out one foot)
(Reaches out one foot)
Good, keep your balance.
Still can't reach it. I wish I didn't skip gym class...
Come on, you can do it!
Argh—
Despite trying her best, the pass still lies just beyond Pepe's reach.
She stretches a little too far and loses her balance.
Following her lead, the mummy lets go of Anat and falls in the same way.
Pepe can only watch as Anat's skull hits the floor, making a dull sound on impact.
(Unconscious cry)
Sorry, Anat, I'll give you my full support on your next research project.
Hanimefendi, do you need help?
Oh, I'm so glad you're here. Your curator isn't feeling well. Can you help me carry her into the office?
Alright.
I set Curator Anat on the sofa. Your posture is... interesting. Are you sure you don't need help getting up?
No, it's a special exercise I do.
Er, okay. What about the exhibit? Should I carry it inside too?
No, I can handle it on my own.
Alright... let me know if you need anything else.
I can finally move... that was close.
Stop staring and come in.
Good news, Narantuya, I found the target.
Yeah, near Hall Four. I helped her carry the unconscious curator into the office.
I'm certain she's in the office. There's no one else, other than the curator who's still out cold. I also have her pass.
No problem, I'll wait here for you.
Izaja?
Oh, Medjehtiqedti Hanimefendi, I...
What are you doing here, with the whole museum in chaos?
We're shutting things down for the day. Help lead these lost guests out.
What's going on? Where are we going?
Are we in danger?
Don't worry, just follow our staff here and she'll take you to a safe place.
B-But I have other things to do...
Alright, I will—
Look out!
Wh-What happened?!
Who brought a cloudbeast in here? Pets aren't allowed in the museum!
I-I didn't see any cloudbeasts...
We'll talk about your negligence later. I've got to catch it before someone steps on it.
...I guess I'm guiding the tourists outside.
(Mutter) Well, I think I can still make it.
Follow me, everyone. The exit is this way, to the right.
Isn't that the left?
She's staff here, I'm sure she knows what she's doing... hey, wait for us!
A heavy exhibition hall door is pushed open, then closed again.
No one remembers that it should be open, no one cares that it was opened, and no one has cleaned the dusty sign that reads 'Northeastern Tributary Exhibit' in ages.
The hall is silent.
Aspasia's heart thumps against her chest, almost loud enough to drown out her whispering footsteps.
She passes by the black monuments of lost tribes and colorful Kjerag rugs, casting her gaze towards the dark depths of the hall.
...Finally.
Our history awaits me in silence.
I must admit that one should look upon history with such solemnity.
From which lost temple's relief was the scroll before me drawn? From which Minoan peak was such immaculately white marble hewn?
And such imagination, piecing together a corner of an ancient Minoan family with ancient artifacts.
But—
It's all wrong!
I recognize you, the golden goblet that once sat in the center of the temple. My grandfather came to visit you, many years ago, as did my mother after him.
Much has my family suffered for your loss.
Why has this museum put it among the clay bowls for fermented milk? They share no common material or style!
And where is the other half of the pair? The Sargonians took them all, yet they do not keep them safe!
Even the exhibit description is—
She takes a long look at the exhibit label, then pulls the metal plate off the wall and looks to the other side.
Both sides are perfectly smooth, with only her own reflection upon them.
Signómi, this one was just left empty.
—But the biggest mistake is that these artifacts are here at all.
A century ago, Minos suffered the shame of Sargonian armies violating her soil, trampling on our civilization and history.
It should not have been so...
I must take them.
Follow this flight of stairs down, and you'll find the exit.
Now then, I have other business to attend to.
Something still doesn't feel right.
I don't think this is how we came in.
She's staff here. She knows what she's doing... right?
Will this way really take us home?
I must take you home.
I must bring you to the priests of Minos, that they may study and preserve you.
Aspasia walks solemnly towards the exhibition pedestal and lifts the goblet.
Minoan artifacts must not be allowed to lie forgotten in this forsaken, sunless hall.
Don't push! I can't see where I'm going. Shouldn't they put an underground light or something in the emergency exit?
Hey, there's a lady there setting up the exhibit. She looks more reliable than the other one. Let's ask her!
Have you come to see—
—Is this the way out of the museum?
Is there something I can help you with?

Let me see inside your mouth. Aaah...
(Opens mouth)
Hmm. I can see structures resembling vocal cords. So why haven't you said a single thing?
Speech is just movement of the vocal cords and associated muscles. You should be able to emulate it...
Pepe approaches the mummy and places her hand on its throat. The mummy follows her movement, placing its hand on Pepe's throat.
You know...
You... know...
Yo... oo... no... oo...

Inconceivable... the artificers of the Shah's time were amazing...
Right. I once read about that in Artifacts of the Old Calendar Era...

Lost... Zhayedan technologies... the Shahanshah will be very interested...
The trove must be somewhere deep inside your memories.
Just copying me isn't enough. You need to tell me where it is.
Let me see...
'First, the heart. At its first beat, we come to be. Second, the lungs. At their first breath, we come to feel.'
'Third, the bowels. We fill them with food, cultivate our tongues, and develop our own likings. Fourth, the liver. We go into the night, awake and alert, for duty and obligation.'
Fifth...
What came fifth?
The verse ends there...
Looks like the Arsalan researcher never found the answer.
But you'll help me find it, won't you?
She stretches out her hand and places her fingers on the mummy's throat.
Yes, hanimefendi.
Yes, hanimefendi.
Stop copying me.
Stop copying me.
She moves her hand from the mummy's throat to his hand and shakes it firmly. The mummy shakes her hand back.
I'll take that as a yes to our cooperation.
I'm here, Ajazi. Why aren't you at the door?
Where are you?
What?!
(Whisper) What do you mean you don't know where you are?
How am I supposed to go in without a pass...? Why is this office door built like a bloody vault?
We don't have time, the evacuation's almost done!
Ajazi, is the hall window next to the curator office's window?
Yes, I know it's high.
I'll be careful.
Please be quiet in the museum. Disorder is the enemy of reason.
Do not push. The statues and murals displayed here are not protected by glass cases and can easily be...

How enlightening and professional...
...But are you sure you need to keep carrying that statue? Along with everything else you're carrying right now?
Yes, I'm bringing it all back to where it belongs.
You sure take your work seriously, even when the museum is about to close.
...Close?

That bumbling woman who was guiding us couldn't tell left from right, and I mean literally.
I do agree that certain affairs at this museum leave much to be desired.

I must dissent. This is no dump. It is Minoan—

Yeah! That's why I never liked museums...
Aspasia catches the tourist before his head hits the floor.

My dear! Are you alright?!
Fainted from a panic attack, I suppose.
I have some smelling salts here that should bring him back to his senses, but I advise you to get him examined thoroughly later.

An emergency, you say?

Follow me, ladies and gentlemen. I will protect you, if anything happens.
Wow...
*sigh* No more running around like this, alright?

—Hey, no scratching! I just rescued you from the crowd! The least you could do is let me hug you for a moment or two.

Oh...

There's a statue in that pose?

Woof!

Woof!

Ugh...

Ugh...
Eek! Get away from me!

Ugh...

We grew up dreaming about inheriting the golden reed pen from our elders.

You are writing the truth of your time, the most complete truth that can be known, which needs no conjecture or investigation and can never be buried by sand and earth.

I want to tell Sargon's history! I want the glory of the Shah to shine forever by my hand, and to inspire everyone who reads it.

Don't you think that's really romantic, Anat?
*Sigh*...

Shouldn't you and Titi have gotten used to my stubbornness by now?

Absolutely! With pleasure!
Pepe...
Yes?

Anat opens the drawer and takes out a wrapped package.

Pepe takes the gem, holding back the urge to cry out in joy.
'Second, the lungs. At their first breath, we come to feel.'
Don't let me down.
Pepe reaches into the mummy's chest cavity. The gem is absorbed by the blue light inside as soon as it approaches.
Guardian, sealed in sand, I hereby return to you the memories lost to time.
Show the path to the Shah's trove, traveler from the past!
It stopped moving after I put in the second gem?
Did... you expect something different?
Eek—!!
That wasn't me. Who's screaming?
It was Titi's voice.
Is something happening?
I'll investigate. Keep an eye on the exhibit.
Why didn't you tell me you were just an ordinary cloudbeast?
Well... I guess ordinary cloudbeasts don't understand human speech.
Looks like all the visitors have been evacuated. It's safe for us to go out now. Do you think you could find your way back to Lazvar if I put you down outside?
Hey, stop hitting me! I'm carrying you so you don't have to walk yourself!
That's right. Awww, such nice fur... come, I'll introduce you to other little friends of yours.
Look at the imagery on this powder box. This cloudbeast is depicted in equal prominence to a high official. I restored this, by the way.
And here. This mural is a rare treasure that came from Shar-Agade, the City of Gold.
In order to accurately restore it, I looked through every book I could find on the Golden City, and even interviewed people who have been there.
Wait. Does that make me sound like I'm after the secrets of Shar-Agade?
Shouldn't think too much about it. You're just a cloudbeast, and there's no one else in the museum. It's not like what I just said would get me in trouble.
Suddenly, she hears the faint sound of cracking.
Don't tell me...
No—! Why did the glass have to pick now to crack?
I'm dead if Anat finds out!
Medjehtiqedti runs toward the display stand, and is shocked by the sight of broken glass littering the floor.
Display cases use specially tempered glass. In the controlled environment of the museum, an accident like this should not have been possible.
She looks up to check on the exhibit, but finds the case empty under the pale light.
She feels a cold, sharp object pressing against her back as she attempts to examine the broken glass.
If this is some kind of joke, you can stop.
I'm too busy for this.
Playing dumb? Which department are you from?
I'm going to dock your pay, if you don't start talking...
Medjehtiqedti feels the object pressed against her back move away. She sighs of relief and turns around.
One of the interns? You've got nerve, playing practical jokes on the vice curator. I'll let you off easy this time...
Eek—!
You... you...
How did you get out of your case?!
Why haven't I heard anything else after that scream? Anat hasn't come back yet... well, I'll just look around.
Anat never changes, does she? She's the acting curator, yet her office still looks so bare and sparse.
She had so many books back in the dorms. Why aren't there any here?
She must be hiding them somewhere...
I knew it. A hidden bookshelf in the wall.
Hmm... My bookshelf at home is due for renovation... I should ask her to introduce me to the designer.
Let's see what you have here... A Study on Tribal Religion in the Southwest, Review of Unearthed Riverside Jewelry...
Terra: A Journey, by Dr. E. E. Erikson? The name sounds familiar... that's the Victorian scholar who came to speak at the college, isn't it?
Lost of labels in here. I didn't know Anat liked his books so much.
Let's check the section on Sargonian history...
(Finally, she's gone.)
(This room sure is luxurious... is that gold thread woven into the cushions on the sofa?)
(That's the mummy from the poster. They put it right here in the room? I'll never understand the tastes of rich people.)
(There she is... good, she's completely distracted...)
(With apologies...)
Narantuya walks up softly and reaches into her pocket, taking out the syringe that she has kept hidden within.
She spent countless days and nights training, even when blisters formed on her feet, in order to be able to walk across a sand dune without leaving any footprints.
Now, she can walk through a crowded market, bells hanging from her neck, without making a single sound.
A Padishah's daughter stands before her. All alone.
When Narantuya raises the syringe, however, a hand suddenly grabs hers and immobilizes it in midair.
(A hidden bodyguard?)
(You—!)
Narantuya cannot speak.
The mummy laying on the sofa has appeared behind her, grabbing her hand with a steely grip.
Her struggles are futile, and all she can do is watch in terror as the needle approaches her own neck.
The other hand of the mummy is clasped across her mouth, not letting go until the entire contents of the syringe have been injected into Narantuya's veins.
As her consciousness gradually fades, she feels the hand lift her by the collar and hold her outside the window.
One last thought flashes through her mind as she feels her feet dangling in the air.
If only she had more competent subordinates.
An insect lurking in the dark.
What...?
What's that sound?
Y-You're moving again?
My mind is no longer clouded. There is clarity. I must communicate my cognition through language.
A-Are you alive?
My life has long passed. What you see is simply a well-preserved vessel.
Who are you?
In times of prosperity, I was His Majesty Lugalszargus Shahanshah's personal guard, defending him from the fires of battle.
In times of decline, I willingly forsook the sweet release of death for the eternal torment of time, defending His Majesty's trove from invaders, safeguarding the Shahanshah's vow, and awaiting...
Awaiting the return of the Shahanshah.
H-How should I call you?
My name is Zubayidamu Yanur Ayyusuiz.
You may call me—Zubayr.
Mother, what is that lady doing sitting by the pond, staring down at the water?
She's dressed in Minoan clothes. The Minoans are said to be great thinkers and debaters.
She must be thinking about deep, philosophical questions...
Philo... what?
But she looks so pretty, sitting there alone...
Hanimefendi, the museum is closed.
I know. I'm waiting for it to reopen.
Well... I'm afraid there's currently no schedule for reopening.
According to procedure, the curator must declare the emergency resolved, perform a risk assessment, and report to the stakeholders before a reopening date can be announced.
I know. One of your colleagues already told me.
As you can see, I'm waiting.
You said you left something in the museum?
Yes.
Is it very urgent? Could you wait for our staff to look for it? We can contact you once we've found it.
No, I can't wait.
How about this... I can unlock the door, and we'll have someone accompany you inside.
Thank you—
Mother...
What is it?
Is someone jumping from that window?!
Aspasia looks up at the child's cry and sees a woman falling from high up.
With little time to think or look to the guard opening the door, she dashes over and holds out her arms.

eps.name: Take Me Out to the Grand Bazaar

lines.x: Titi...
Fetch!
(Fetches the ball)
(Brings it back)
Who's the cutest little thing? You are!
Stop fooling around, Titi!
I've always wanted a pet, but my family wouldn't let me have one. Look how cute it is!
It's not a pet, its part of the museum's collection.
Tell that to Pepe. She's yelling at part of the museum's collection.
SAY THAT AGAIN?
I apologize, Pepe Khanum, but I am unable to answer. My memories of the trove's location remain vague.
You sure you don't want to stop her? I'm starting to feel sorry for that corpse cowering in a corner.
Well, try harder! Maybe you'll remember then.
I'm afraid that would be futile...
They who split my memories distributed them evenly across all the gems, to ensure that the secret could not be easily accessed.
There are five gems in total. You may have to find all of them before I can tell you the answer.
Where are the rest of the gems? You must know that, right?
(Shakes head)
You don't?
The army that defeated me consisted of many padishahs and lords ameer... They took the gems with them when they left.
It's like looking for a needle in a haystack...
There must be another way! Use that golden brain of yours!
Zubayr is forced into a corner by Pepe, even as the short girl doesn't come up to his chest.
He feels a pressure so great, it threatens to buckle even his hardened metal kneecaps.
Please, hanimefendi... have patience...
Then tell me something useful!
There is a faint resonance between the gems that I can sense if I am in their proximity.
Are you kidding me? You want me to take you across all of Sargon? This city alone holds thousands of gems!
I think I sense one faintly...
Well, what are we waiting for? Let's go!
Look, Anat, she's trying to remove part of the museum's collection!
Pepe, Zubayr Effendi is a valued part of the museum's collection. We must ensure that he is stored under appropriate conditions, and I can't allow him to be exposed to the elements.
Leave that thought and do your research here. Do NOT go out.
Forty-percent increase in funding next year?
No.
Double?
My staff will be watching you at all times. Senebty.
What's come over her?
Zubayr Effendi, I know it's not exactly polite to ask a man for his funeral effects, but I would be remiss...
Can I borrow this for a couple of days?
(Spins rapidly)
Of course, hanimefendi.
You can come out now, Zubayr. There's no one here.
How do you go through a hole like that at your size? It's like you turned liquid or something.
My body is extremely flexible, allowing me to pass through narrow spaces.
I thought I would have to tear down the wall, if you couldn't get out.
There is no need.
Zubayr closes his eyes and shakes his sistrum.
As the chimes ring, the wall around the hole slowly turns to sand, falling to the ground, leaving behind a much bigger hole.
With eyes wide, Pepe walks back and forth through the hole, then picks up a handful of sand.
Why didn't you use your Originium Arts? I almost hit my head crawling through the hole.
You didn't ask.
Well... you should close it back up. Wouldn't want anyone else to see it.
As you wish.
The chimes ring again, and sand flows back into the hole. Soon, there is no sign that the hole was ever there.
Centuries ago, there was nothing but sand. Now, tall buildings line the streets. Such prosperity.
Lamps line the banks of the river flowing gently through the city. It reminds me of Shar-Cheshmeh, built upon an oasis.
Shahr-Cheshmeh, the City of Springs? I thought it was just a legend. No one has found it in centuries.
No, it was very real. Its padishah was a despot who challenged the Shah's authority on multiple occasions. I had to pay him a personal visit.
And then?
And then it was no more.
Did... the whole city have to pay for the sins of a single padishah?
I did nothing but cut the padishah's head off and throw it into a spring. It was the chained slaves who burned the city to the ground.
If you visited yourself, you would be shocked by the filth and stink. They made sacrifices in the water, but the water system was... primitive.
Please, stop. I can almost smell it.
I see. Can we go to the river?
No, not that way!
It's early. The banks of the river are still brightly lit. Anat would kill me if someone saw the museum's collection walking the streets.
I know a place. Follow me.
Curator Anat, we've taken inventory of the collection. Here it is...
...Medjehtiqedti Khanum? What are you doing here?
Anat went to meet a guest, so I'm taking over some of the documents.
'The Padishah's statue wasn't damaged in an accident; it was missing one eyeball to begin with! Who wrote this?'
Ha...
How's my impression?
But it's true that the statue's records are wrong. Be careful next time.
R-Really? Let me see who was in charge.
Izaja and Inaja. I think they were under your...
Ahem... here, Age of Shadow exhibits counted twice. Make sure you double-check.
This is...?
My favorite place in the city.
You like empty streets.
It's not like this during the day. Everything in Menat-Ha'mait goes through here.
Merchants from all across the country come here to trade gems, furs, spices, crafts... people come from other countries too, often making large transactions.
If we're going to find a gem somewhere, this would be it.
Deep in the Grand Bazaar is a trading post dedicated to gems. Virtually every gem in the city passes through it.
Do you sense the gem we're looking for?
Are you listening?
Forgive me...
I am curious about the crafts being sold here. In my time, animal sculptures like these were guardians of tombs, companions for the dead.
Now, though, they are being peddled on the streets as everyday items.
Well... it's a decorative sculpture for home use. Twenty-five dinars.
From the murals on the walls, to the hieroglyphs all around... many things feel so familiar, yet so strange at the same time.
Perhaps we should go and see a few more places?
Medjehtiqedti Hanimefendi, the great families have sent inquiries.
The padishahs take the solemnity of Sargonian history so seriously. How do we explain this incident to them?
Just tell them it was an intern's mistake and we'll deal with it accordingly.
Deal with whom?
The acting curator, perhaps?
...Huh?
Is it really true, Titi?
There was unexplained access made using your pass both last night and this morning... what if someone investigates?
That can't be! I wiped all the records clean.
So you've finally admitted it.
I didn't... I...
Well, this I can cover up... but the mummy coming back to life, on the eve of the Flood Festival, is another matter.
What's your plan?
To keep the museum closed until things blow over.
No way!
Look at that badgir!
A windcatcher, yes, the most common type of tower here. Those openings catch the cool, high winds and guide them into the building through vents.
Cool air flows into the space below, pushing the hot air out the opposite side.
I didn't know you were an architect.
I'm not. But I met the talented architect who invented the badgir.
Do you sense the gems yet?
I'm sorry... I can't sense any nearby. Maybe I could tell you something else.
Are you not curious how the gems retain one's memory and consciousness?
Well...
I AM curious. I figured it was some kind of Sarkaz witchcraft related to the Zhayedan.
People in my time believed that life consists of three parts: Body, Name, and Soul.
Body is the physical vessel, and Name is the identity known to others. The Soul, however, is what truly makes us human; it is the emotions and experiences that form our character.
All three parts must be preserved together, in order for life to be eternal.
The Body can be replaced with something more durable. The Name can retain its meaning by being passed on through generations.
I know. That's the genesis of Sargonian noble heritage.
The Soul, however, is the frailest of the three.
During the Age of Shadow, it was discovered that part of the Soul can be preserved, in a manner of speaking, by etching it upon a pure gem with Originium circuits.
Like the gem in your chest...
Yes.
I've only ever read about it in ancient texts... Scholars call it superstition, borne out of a lack of understanding of the nature of life.
That is a misinterpretation.
However, the true mystery, the method by which a life's journey is engraved, has long since been lost to time.
Or, it might exist, to some degree... gems with engraved Originium circuits can still be found in this city.
Last year, a gem with Originium circuits that could change the ambient temperature in a small area was sold at auction for an incredible price.
Some things survive the test of time, only to become barely recognizable.
The sun's about to rise. We should get back before the museum opens. Let's go.
The museum is just ahead. I don't want to crawl through another hole. Why don't we use your Arts to make a shortcut?
One moment, Pepe.
Have you sensed the gem?
No...
I would like to see the sunrise.
The one thing that the passage of time cannot change.
Okay. Just for a bit.
How lucky. It is still bright, glorious, brilliant.
It still brings warmth.
Inudi harek, Horakhet.
Zubayr slumbered for centuries beneath the sands, with only darkness for company.
He squints straight at the sun rising in the east, the brilliant wheel of flame.
Sunlight shines upon him for the first time in a very long while, illuminating his golden body.
Pepe can barely open her eyes, so bright it is.
Is it time to go back?
You can stay a while longer, if you like being in the sun.
We have a little bit of time.
The sun is up.
Four days... no, five, since Narantuya went missing.
Focus, Ajazi! This work requires attention to detail!
You're sure that the communicator fell into this drainage outlet when we left the museum, right?
I'm sure.
Well, we've been dredging through it every day, but we haven't found a shadow of it.
We can't reach Narantuya if we don't find it. if we can't reach Narantuya... what will we do?
...I remember Narantuya saying we should find a way to get money when she's not around.
You're right, Ajani!
We need to pull a big job.
That way, we can buy a new communicator and get in touch with the boss!
Let's go. We've got work to do at the museum.

Alright...
Huh?
Hey, Ajazi, I think I hooked something!
What do you think, Lazvar?
A hue like the night sky, and the wavy patterns it like lava gently flowing across it...
It was dug up from the deep Obsidian veins decades ago. It's a rare treasure today, given the state of Mount Siesta.
The gem vendors in town dare not give a hasty quote for it, and you've been away from the post these past few days...
There is no Originium circuit on this gem.
I'm afraid I cannot give you a high price for it.
The blue fowlbeast that was perched on the overseer's staff flies up, grabs the Obsidian, and places it gently on the scale.
The scale comes to a halt, and the young overseer looks at the reading.
Three hundred and seventy dinars.
B-But a Siestan collector offered a much higher price for it! The equivalent of thirty-seven thousand dinars!
Could you perhaps be mistaken?
You could always go back to that collector.
Ah, er, well, it's not that I don't trust the overseer's eyes and scales...
But if we were in Londinium, Lungmen, or Siesta...
We're in Menat-Ha'mait.
There are treasures here beyond reckoning. The sand beneath your feet was perhaps at one time gems brighter than the stars.
That is why we value the power of a gem far more than its appearance.
Three hundred and seventy dinars is the most I can give for an ordinary Obsidian, with no Originium circuits or special powers.
Can we really sell a rock that we reeled in from the sewers?
Even the roads in this city are paved with gems. Try holding it under the sun.
What am I supposed to see?
Nothing. It's like the plastic handle of my toothbrush.
Well, don't be discouraged, Ajani. It could help Narantuya's great endeavor, even if it's not worth much.
Like, she could enjoy ice water for an entire week once she comes back. Added with lemon slices and dried dates for sure.
Hey, you! Yeah, you, behind the counter.
Me?
Yeah. You're the receptionist, right?
Receptionist? No—
It's our first time here. We want to... to... what do we want to do, Ajazi?
To get this gem appraised and sell it for good money!
Yeah, that's right. Do you know where the queue for gem appraisals is?
...Give it to me. I can appraise it.
Wow, even the receptionist at the gem trading post knows how to appraise gems!
At first glance, it is a pale sapphire of low-purity.
Low-purity? Sounds like dried dates are off the menu.
Well, the lemon slices still have a chance.
The Originium circuits are clearly visible. Hm? This is...
What is it? Is there something wrong with the circuits?
Meh, never mind, Ajazi. The gem probably doesn't do anything, judging by his reaction.
...No, it does. The Originium circuits on this gem can induce excitement in small animals.
It makes small animals excited...?
Where are you going, Ajani?
Back to work at the museum, of course! You know how much money we're making per hour carrying boxes?
Forget that blue toothbrush handle! Can't afford to waste our time here!
Hey, wait for me!
Wait, your gem—
I was going to say I could offer fifty thousand dinars for it...
Meow...
Mio? You're back early.
(Dejected) Meow...
...It's okay, you tried. It's not easy to see him, is it?
Hey, Mio, try this.
(Touches the pale blue gem with a paw)
(Sniffs)
(Purrs excitedly)
(Rolls in ecstasy)
Glad you like it... what's that? You found your motivation again?
Alright, then, go with Woof to find the gems.
They've finally converged upon this city.

eps.name: Take Me Out to the Grand Bazaar

lines.x: Shh, stay in the grass and don't come out. I had to be real careful to get you out during the day.
The grass is too short. It can't hide my ears.
Then get lower!
Uh...
Father, that girl is rummaging in the grass.
Shh, it's not polite to point. She must be drunk.
No! I can still see them!
She's about to fall into the grass. Should we help?
Shh, pretend not to see her. A little fall never hurt anyone. It's being seen falling that's really embarrassing.
This is where the events leading up to the Flood Festival are held, and all the competitors are here today to sign up. It would be very awkward to run into her during the competition.
It's a Menat-Ha'mait tradition to sink various items into the river as a sacrifice to the river god ahead of the Flood Festival, praying for good luck.
Guess where we can find the rarest gems in the city.
In the river.
Wait here while I go get one of the bush trimming carts. You can sit in the bin for collecting fallen leaves.
That way, we can get you to the river without anyone finding out!
Are you sure this is a good idea?
Come on, stop tugging at my shirt and act your age!
Stay hidden and stay put! I'll be back soon!
Meow... meooow...
A... creature?
(Lifts tail elegantly)
Ah, many statues here were carved in your likeness.
(Wags tail proudly)
Beautiful. No wonder the people here love you so much.
(Holds up backside with dignity)
Do you want me to pet you? But you're too far, and Pepe told me not to move from here.
(Makes a deliberate display)
Can you come closer?
(Shakes tail irritably)
Please don't be angry. Perhaps I can move forward a little.
Meow...
Meow!
Wait!
Hey, Zubayr, stop gawking at cloudbeasts. Look what I've found!
...Why do you look so dour?
I am not dour.
Then get in here. I've measured it, it should fit you with room to spare.
It's a little dirty...
Dirty? Just some fallen leaves and a piece of scrap paper. You were dug out of a hole filled with sand and dirt!
...I respectfully decline.
Alright, alright, I'll clean it. Fallen leaves... scrap paper... it's a leaflet. It's not even that dirty.
Pepe spreads open the rolled-up ball of paper.
She freezes on the spot.
Harvest Gathering? What is that?
Part of the local festivities... competitors display their most valued possessions, and the winners get prizes from the sponsors.
Many of the prizes are familiar. The second-place prize, in particular, brings back memories.
Of course it does, Zubayr.
Do I still have to go into the bin?
Of course. I cleaned it for you.
Mmh...
(Mumbles in sleep)
This way... the mummy won't...
...Whoa!
Is she finally awake?
—Damn. I'm glad that was just a dream.
I don't want to be haunted by the dead this young.
Please don't throw the vessel. Even if it is your own, you may not harm others while using it.
Sorry. I thought it was my knife I was throwing.
Apology accepted. Good morning.
Wait, morning?
In the silence, Narantuya sizes up the stranger before her, who gives off the aura of a seasoned warrior.
She slowly moves her hand to her belt, while carefully taking in her surroundings.
Her coin purse is still tied to her belt, but her weapons are gone.
Her knives are lying on the tea table, on the other side of this unfamiliar room.
The room itself seems unremarkable, but this is a city where even the manhole covers are laid with gems.
Narantuya knows that even the most unremarkable thing here can command a remarkable price.
Oh... it was you who saved my life?
Thank you so much, kind soul! Once I get out of here, I'll have all my subordinates remember your name!
So...
How much do you want?
I'm sorry?
If you mean the bread and fruit that I brought back from my morning jog, these were gift from others that should not be valued in terms of money.
I wasn't asking about that, or why I don't get good stuff like that when I walk the streets.
—Wait, you mean it's free?
Good. The sun is my witness, no taking back your word.
I appreciate the kindness.
How are you feeling?
Based on my medical knowledge, I do not believe you're injured, but you had been unconscious for a long time...
Oh, I'd forgotten to sleep for a while. Guess I was making up for it.
No worries. Consider it a... bad hereditary habit.
Hereditary?
As long as it's nothing serious. Feeling unwell anywhere?
She reaches for her neck instinctively.
The needle wound has healed, but the memories from before the long nightmare begin to float to the surface.
The pass that never arrived, the failed kidnapping, the terrifying mummy, the feeling of freefall, the faint sound of wind.
That was the tenth floor... I didn't think I would ever wake up from that.
Wow, is that what dying feels like? It's terrible! Thank you for saving my life.
They say that one can never truly die, if her name continues to be spoken after her death.
But if eternal life means an eternal nightmare of being chased by mummies to the ends of the sands... then I must reconsider whether it's worth it to have my name known all across Sargon.
You jumped from the window because you wanted to have your name known all across Sargon?
Hm? I suppose.
I fail to understand the Sargonian obsession with such tradition... I sympathize. Had things not been going well for you in life?
Racked up a debt. It's been a pretty big headache.
Say, I want to ask you...
You saved my life, and I ought to repay you.
Do you want to work for me? I'm planning something of a great endeavor.
What?
I remember now. I made a wish when I fell from the building.
I guess you're that 'competent subordinate' granted by fate.
Very well. I'm happy to be of help, if it alleviates your stress and gives you the will to live.
Anat! Anat! Look what I found!
I don't know what you found, but I found that you've been sneaking away with the museum's collection again.
And putting him in a dirty... leaf bin.
Sorry, I just couldn't bear to watch him waste away in the museum, so I took him out to get some fresh air.
Is that so?
Er... yes, Anat Hanim, I was interested in seeing the outside.
See? Let's drop this and talk about the good stuff I found.
Hm... a creased leaflet?
Look what it says!

The typical pre-festival events.
Wait, is that...?
The third gem that Zubayr and I have been looking for!
Didn't you say you took Zubayr out because he was wasting away in the museum?
Kill two fowlbeasts with one stone, as they say.
Did you think about how to get the gem? Second-place is not exactly easy to get.
The Harvest Gathering attracts the rich and noble, eager to show off their treasures. I know of at least two padishahs who are visiting anonymously.
I can help you appraise ancient artifacts, or volunteer myself as the entry...
No worries, that's the easy part.
The hard part is finishing second.
The entry has to be extraordinary, but not unique. It must represent an era, but not symbolize it.
You need finish exactly second to get the 'large gem with unique characteristics and Shah-era markings'.
How to finish second...? That is the question.
Heheh.
Do you have an idea?
Pepe does not answer. She looks up at the skylight, squinting.
It is past noon, and sunlight spills through the round skylight, casting an oval of light on the ground.
Standing in the middle of the oval, Pepe grins from ear to ear, pointing at the sun outside.
Heknu, Horakhet.
Entering with a Horakhet statue? Not a bad choice.
No, I'm just expressing my gratitude for His inspiration. I'm not entering an item associated with Him.
Look at the circle He's casting on the ground. Doesn't it look like...?
I see. You plan to enter with a talisman engraved with a royal name. The cartouche is the symbol of good fortune and warding against evil. Perhaps that will catch the attention of the judges...
What are you people talking about?
Isn't this oval of light just like a golden slipper?
Slipper?
Yes. I'm winning that gem with a golden slipper that's been passed down through my family for generations!
I don't want to keep crouching behind this wall. Can I go in with you?
Stop complaining. I got you your ice cream. Can you actually taste it though?
No... I'm just curious what all those people were queuing for.
Is your curiosity satisfied? Eat up, or your head is going to melt in the sun.
Ice treat created in my image... I'm surprised how popular I am here.
Hm...
You may not be able to taste it, but you can feel the coolness, right?
Not so much a sensation as an impulse...
An impulse to jump into the river, allow the water to flow over me, and have a good swim.
That's just how we normally spend the summer, isn't it? Oh right... gold sinks in water.
That's why I described it as an impulse.
Alright, alright, be patient, and I'll take you to the largest artificial lake in town once the sun goes down and the competition has ended.
I'll be going. See you later.
(Pulls up cape)
♪The papyrus reeds sway by the river, its waters of purest lazurite♪
♪It wends its way by the golden dunes, fed by our gifts of gems♪
I knew you were here when I smelled synthesized courmarin.
My watch tells me the competition doesn't start for another thirty minutes. I thought it was unfashionable in the new city to arrive on time.
Twenty-seven minutes and fifty seconds, to be precise. My watch is more accurate.
After all, its mechanism is based on the machine that the Shah of Past and Future invented after he discovered the artifact that gave mastery over time.

Your idea of time is as vague as that sundial in the museum. You do not understand that the every grain of sand passing through the hourglass is a gold coin, when one arrives too early and has to wait.
True, I believe this antique was last calibrated when the sundial was dug up. I wonder where your ancestors were, at the time.
Perhaps I should inform them that it's actually 11:25, and both of them should adjust their watches.
This sports watch is waterproof and dustproof, and has a compass on it. I wear it on my monthly climb of the Heria Mountains, and I can recommend it to them based on firsthand experience.
Don't bother them. You got some free time, go try some other drinks.
I like this iced guava juice more than the herbal tea.
I'll get a few more cups if you want.
I'm fine, I'm no padishah, I know how to use my hands.
So... you're not contemplating suicide?
No.
Why would I, when you can get all sorts of free drinks just walking through a city garden like this—
If I may say so, my hundred-thousand dinar burdenbeast pulls the carriage faster. It could knock a grown man down with its wake.
If I may say so, stability is the most important thing, and that's what the burdenbeasts of my ranch offer.
Oh!—Novsh! My cup!
Unlike you, I don't have to pull down the curtains to stop sand sent flying by a carriage, or juice spilled by passersby.
What are these people doing here? This place is for drinks!
Don't worry, I caught it. Not a single drop of guava juice was spilled.
Let's find a place to sit by the roadside.
(Mutters) She's the competent subordinate I've been wishing for alright... but am I even the boss, at this rate?
(Mutters) I should introduce her to Ajazi and Ajani.
Did you say something?
No, I was just thinking I would take a trip to the museum after I've tried all the drinks here.
You're going there too?
Yeah, but I'm not as interested as you in the artifacts. I just lost something and I need to start searching from the last place I saw it.
Not a single beep from my communicator after all this time? Not one call?
Unfortunately, the museum has remained closed ever since the incident, and I doubt the management would promptly contact the owners of lost items as promised.
Whenever I close my eyes, I think back to the day when I glimpsed a fragment of the lost glory of ancient Minos, and the disarray within that grand museum.
Setting everything aside, it is unbelievable that the staff could not recall the locations of the exhibit halls and the routes to them.
Leading dozens of panicking tourists to the Northeast... to the Minoan exhibit, where there were no exits.
I must strongly express my dissatisfaction when I have a chance to meet with museum management.
Ha... I changed my mind. Let's not go to the museum today.
Excuse me. I see you're enjoying the drinks we have on offer today.
The two of you have lingered here for a while. Perhaps you are interested in participating in the Harvest Gathering?
You appear to be tourists. This pamphlet will give you details about this key event leading up to the Flood Festival.
Not interested.
Let's go, Aspasia... Aspasia?
Fate has smiled on me at last.
Narantuya, you say you have visited the museum. Perhaps you know what this is.
No idea... isn't it just a simple old cup?
This is my family goblet!
The other goblet, to be precise.
Oh, what an honor it is for me to meet you here, Hatshepsut Hanimefendi.

The slipper from the early New Calendar era you brought has certainly broadened my knowledge of history. I had no idea the people of so many centuries ago had such fine techniques in crafting footwear.
To think they could make a shoe that fits the curve of the foot so well. As for the lotus ornament atop it, vivid beyond words.
Not to mention this slipper was made not long after the great explosion, and it reflects the drastic shifts in the local climate.
It's a pity that it's missing its twin. I fear it won't win on its own.
That's fine, second place is enough for me. It's the participation that counts.
Of course, of course. As daughter of the Padishah, Hatshepsut Hanimefendi, you will one day take up the mantle of Padishah. I am sure such a trifling honor would not be worth fretting over.
That's... still a bit early. There's a lot of things we can't say for sure.
I've heard your younger brother has been selected to become court historian, and that he will set out for the City of Gold in two months, Hatshepsut Hanim.
Ah, even a girl as talented as Hatshepsut Hanim was not chosen for the post, which just goes to show how gifted her brother is.
*sigh* We're practically the same, really.

Are you trying to say, good sir, that becoming a padishah does not require talent and ability? I believe that if Hatshepsut Hanim were to take that position, her family would no doubt continue to bask in glory.
This is different. Hatshepsut Hanim's family is Sargon's most illustrious line of historians, after all.
Almost time for us to register. Have you thought of what to submit?
Not yet.
You so brazenly got into line, but you don't have a single treasure on you?
Is there anything about my walking posture that needs improvement?
Never mind, forget it.
I guess you'll have to rely on me again.
I'm not about to let my subordinate lose. Give me some time to think of a plan.
I once won the silver medal in a sprint.
Are you thinking of snatching the golden cup and running? No good.
These rich folk brought along some tough bodyguards. See the way their feet move as they look around? They've spent time in the ameeri armies.
I also earned a silver medal in an unarmored wrestling.
Robbery? Everyone worth targeting is already here. How would we participate with stolen goods without getting caught?
There is also the silver medal I got from a ceremonial dance competition.
That's your plan of attack?
*sigh* No need to make things hard for yourself...
In truth, I have ten silver medals across ten different events. I keep them on my person at all times.
...Ten silver medals?
These medals are embodiments of traditional Minoan culture, and my most prized honors.
Honor should not be regarded as a rarity with which to judge superiority, but in particular circumstances, I have no other choice.
Besides, I hope you do not misunderstand me, Narantuya. I would never resort to anything dishonorable. It would disgrace my family.
I guess I really did misunderstand you.
*sigh* My competent subordinate, my great endeavor...
Sorry, did you say something just now?
