Nothing important. I was just saying that considering you've never won a gold medal, it's no wonder you want that gold cup, haha.
Don't worry. I have a plan.
*cough*...
May I ask... why the herald led me here? What orders am I to receive?
Eh? No, no, no orders here. Just a request.
If you happen to see a single golden slipper... Do not give it too high a mark.
Ah, but also not too low! We must still uphold the professionalism of our historians! Let's say... second place. Second place would be good.
Please! This is really, really important!
That exhibit has precious historical value, but if you wish to reduce its mark for subjective reasons, that would not be considered unfair to our other competitors.
If you have deduced my identity, then please keep that a secret as well. Thank you.
I will, resplendent Hanim.
...Sorry.
I'm definitely going to apologize to all my teachers and fellow archaeologists after this.
I... I definitely, absolutely, 100% cannot lose, just this once! Why does everyone think I already lost to that little brat?
AIIEEEEEEEE———!
(*covers mouth*) ...Did I scare him?
Did you get all that?
Y-Yes! Th-The set of silver medals from M-Minos... must get a high mark... It will get th-third place...
That's right.
Don't worry, I already had a talk with most of your fellow judges. We got along rather well. I hope you will too.
Ah... Padishah Effendi... The corrupt must be hanged? F-Forgive me!
(Can't believe he's this scared already! I didn't get this big a reaction from security and the other judges earlier. What illusion did he see?)
(*sigh* I'll be dragging you back to the gate, then. Maybe I overdid it with the Arts?)
(Wait. I hear footsteps.)
Oh, I don't see the judge here either. Must have been escorted back to the meeting hall by security.
I better go get my own things done. The sun waits for no man, after all.
Oho, and who is this I happen to run into? What luck.
Sorry, could you say that one more time?
I am honored to inform you, hanimefendi, that your entry has proceeded to the final round of judging. In other words, you are now in the top three.
No, not that. The other part.
I ask that you follow me to the riverside in a little while, where the prizes will be given and the treasures will be immersed into the river.
So... You're going to throw my ten silver medals into the river?
Perish the thought, hanimefendi. That would be rather crude, and we would never dare to offend the river god.

We will place the treasures atop a small boat made of papyrus reeds, alongside gold, silver, and jewels to help weigh it down. We will sing hymns and chants as we watch the boat slowly sink into the waters.
This wasn't mentioned on the brochure.

I am terribly sorry. It was written in the instructions all participants had to sign. The competition's original purpose was to find treasures with the most historical value to knock upon the gate of the god.
During the Flood Festival, those favored winners will witness the river god with their own eyes as He wanders the riverside.
Forgive me, this was my mistake. It was close to the cutoff when I signed up, and I did not have time to read all ten pages of instructions.
Ah, that is a shame. As it is extremely rare for tourists to reach the final round of selection, you will be given a second opportunity to reaffirm your commitment.
Do you wish to continue participating?
Narantuya...
She hasn't returned yet.
Do you wish to seek a specialist consultant? I can bring you to the chief judge to discuss—
No need. What I seek is not a suggestion.
I will continue my participation.
Hello? Anat, listen to me! You can start preparing the victory feast now!
Hm-hm, of course, I would never take for granted the generosity you’ve shown by lending me the general!
I promise you that I'll stick the gem in when we get back tonight. If Zubayr goes through any changes, you'll be able to see them for yourself.
...Relax, I've been taking good care of him. No stains or damage, I promise.
...Y-You want to speak to him? No, nonono, he... he's under my protection, right? How can I just give him a communicator when everyone's staring at me?
Why don't we talk about something else?! You don't know how hard I've had it! I've been talking up these old pedantic judges all morning just to guarantee second place.
Listen, they're about to declare the winner at the big meeting spot. Once I get the gem, I'll head right back to the museum, where it's nice and quiet—
Sorry.
Ah, it's fine...
...Weird, not like the sidewalk's narrow here.
*phew* I can finally rest easy. Pepe's handled the gem issue, and Zubayr is just fine too.
Should we have a drink together later?
Is there something you wanted to say?
You're not going to want to hear it.
I do want to hear your suggestion, Titi.
You might think this is ridiculous, but... I feel like we might have to start the exhibition over, and keep the accident under wraps.
Just treat it as some sort of performance gone awry, then display it in a more vivid and... true-to-life manner?
So display a walking mummy and some artifacts, push up ticket prices a little while we're at it?
More or less, I guess...
You do know what our people use to scare crybaby children, right?
The exhibits being a bit odd isn't a bad thing!
My old Columbian museum had an exhibit: 'Has Science Gone Too Far? Creative Ideas That Would Be Scarier Successes Than Failures'.
But Titi, that's the idea behind a business whose number one goal is to make money. If we tried that here, a lot of people would be mad. A lot of people.
History itself is the foundation of this city!
Isn't the foundation that vein of gemstones that's nearly dried up?
You really shouldn't say things like that when we go to meet the nobles later.
That family is as ancient as the bricks in the tomb in the museum's great hall. They can be rather conservative.
Meddling here and there... What's wrong with those high-ups anyway?! Can't they just fork over a sum of money every year and forget about the museum like Pepe's family?
I worked hard to become acting curator, Titi. I really don't want to cause any sort of inconvenience.
Inconvenience?! You're seriously calling this an 'inconvenience'?!
That's not what I meant...
Have it your way, then. I'm leaving.
Titi, I...
(Are you okay? You're sweating buckets...)
(A-Am I? It's rather hot outside, haha. You're sweating too...)
Next up is a descendant of a glorious clan of historians, the one who shares ballads of kings and heroes, explorer of all Sargon, purveyor of knowledge, a respected Padishah's daughter—
Hatshepsut Hanim and her rare treasure!
(Are you, err... Are you going to announce the result?)
(Oh, no, this is a rather important occasion. I leave it to you.)
(...Haha, well, I guess I can't get out of this one.)
Ahem...

Uh, this golden slipper is adorned with a lotus flower woven of gold thread, specially designed to accommodate the sudden changes in the Sargonian climate at the time, with distinct characteristics from the era.
Not only that, but it comes from an illustrious family of imperial historians.
It would be difficult to find a slipper so well-preserved in the marketplace, and it's a submission rather uncharacteristic of a family of historians, at that—
For past ceremonies, such families would usually submit letters, manuscripts, and other pieces of writing.
But today, this sole slipper reveals to us another side of the life of a noble past. Its value is incalculable!

Okay, thank you for the commentary. Next, I will announce the judges' decision.
Disregarding the highest and the lowest marks, Hatshepsut Hanim's final tally is...
—94.95 points.
Ah, what a shame, resplendent Hatshepsut Hanim, off by a mere 0.05 points. You are in second place, for now...
This result was born in the interests of fairness and our respect for history. I do hope you understand, Hatshepsut Hanim.
Oh, don't worry about it. I expected as much, heheh.
Menat-Ha'mait is honored to undertake your challenge... L-Let's move on to the next item, shall we?
Very well. Next, we have the last, but certainly not the least, entry into our competition...
A foreign treasure, a set of silver medals from Minos. We invite our expert to give an assessment.
We are all aware that places far from Sargon also possess their own brilliant civilizations. The art and culture of Minos has proven to be harmonious with Sargonian social mores.
This set of medals embodies not only the Minoan love of sports, but more importantly, unlike most of the other entries, its year of manufacture can be determined with precision.
Indeed, our lives are well-adorned with gold, but products fashioned from silver are a rarity.
In fact, some historical periods saw silver considered the more precious metal.

What a fascinating analysis! Well then, which three contestants will win the opportunity to represent the city in offering these treasures to the river? Do allow me to announce this particular treasure's score...
Disregarding the highest and the lowest marks, hailing from Minos, Aspasia Hanim's final tally is...
—94.60 points.

This level of fierce competition is unprecedented! Your final position is third place, Aspasia Hanim. Though I regret you did not place higher, you have still secured the chance to perform the offering.
I thank you. This was precisely the approval I sought.
However, if you so permit, I would protest your arrangement of these prizes after we conclude. I believe a golden Minoan goblet should rank higher than a third place prize.
Very well, Hanim, but please come up to the stage first with your treasure. Go with our attendant there.
In third place, Aspasia Hanim.
In second place, Hatshepsut Hanim.
In first place, Rashad Hanim.
I ask that our three winning contestants take care not to damage the treasures they are holding—
Oh no!
Terribly sorry about that. It's far too hot, and I collapsed for a moment... I couldn't hold it steady. This is quite embarrassing.
*sigh* It's a pity this ancient clay pot I brought shattered to pieces. I assume that offering pottery chips would be an affront to the river god?
I... don't believe this has ever happened before...
This is heartbreaking. I fear I will have to give up first place.
Resplendent Hatshepsut Hanim, daughter of the Padishah, you hold the highest title among all attendees. To bestow first place upon you is only fitting.
And so I voluntarily renounce my position and withdraw from the competition.
...Huh?
Err... What an unexpected turn of events! But I do believe everyone is in agreement that our resplendent daughter of the Padishah should be awarded first place!
And so, our second, third and fourth place contestants will become our new first, second, and third place respectively.
Let's all give them our heartiest congratulations!

lines.st: ♪The papyrus reeds sway by the river, its waters of purest lazurite♪
♪It wends its way by the golden dunes, fed by our gifts of gems♪
♪May I see Him by the waterside, may I follow Him to eternity♪

lines.x: Amidst the ardent applause and passionate singing, the winners stiffly walk along the shining, bejeweled street.
A reed boat bearing the heavy weight of history slowly sinks into the river. Golden slipper and Minoan silver medal alike become no more than a string of bubbles.
Regret? How could I possibly have any regrets? Meeting you has been the best thing to happen to me this entire competition, Hatshepsut Hanim!
What worth would a competition ranking hold compared to such privilege...? If I may be so bold, I believe a group photo of us losers with the champion would be fittingly commemorative.
Would you do me the honor of having a photo with me, resplendent Hatshepsut Hanim? If you would hold the first-place prize statue as well, that would be perfect.
Haha, of course... Thank you for your kind words...
I joined in a most absurd competition.
Yeah, that was absurd indeed.
I intended to seek history, honor the feats of heroes past, be steadfast in my pursuit of victory, and confront fate itself.
Yeah, confronting fate and... the other thing. Basically, your ten old silver medals are a single shiny new one.
But don't worry. This is Sargon, after all, so that's no failure.
'Golden sands above, gold itself below.' As the lords ameer and padishahs say, sand can be as precious as gold.
Don't sweat it. Maybe you'll go down some windy road, you'll find yourself recognized, and that golden cup will be handed right over.
Handed over?
Let's say, maybe you were to chat with the lucky winner of the cup, and wheedle your way into his trust...
—You're right!
Huh?
The treasure I put forth was recognized. This means that Minoan culture itself was also recognized.
Even if the assessment of the judges was strikingly superficial, the scoring of the silver medals and my position are sufficient proof that even Sargonian historians hold a basic respect for Minos.
I should have words with the collector of the goblet. If he were to understand the deep history behind it, perhaps he would be willing to sell it back to me at a price I can afford.
You call that recognition?
Well, if it's enough to cheer you up, I guess we'll call that a victory.
Thank you very much, Narantuya. You are too kind.
Just like the wise men debating around the white columns, you have awoken me. I will not lose the honor that those silver medals represented. In fact, I have now won even more in turn.
The trick played upon me by fate is unworthy of notice, by comparison.
Moreover, if immersing those medals in the water would aid the people to see what they wish to see, then I am happy to serve.
If throwing this gem into the river would summon the god I've heard so much talk of, then I—
WAIT!
I can see you're feeling a bit better, but calm down. Don't throw it away.
I have a plan... Or I guess, I have a person I wish to see.
If you don't really care about the gem, could I... borrow it for a while?
The traps are ready.
Relax, I was real careful putting them together. The more someone knows, the less they'll suspect.
Good.
I also slipped the invitation into the girl's bag. I made sure to wear gloves for handling artifacts, no way I left any fingerprints behind.
Nice work.
I'll handle the back end of our plan. Any questions from you two?
I have one! I don't understand why the Padishah's daughter would fall for our bait?
This gem doesn't seem that special. It's got lots of impurities, like the stone Ajani and I pulled up from the sewer the other day...
Don't worry about what the rich folk think.
Watch the door tonight. Keep your eyes peeled. Don't let her send anyone over to take it before the time is right.
Yeah, okay... We won't let you down this time.
That's right! We won't let anyone in!
Meow?
Mio arrives before the table, having deftly avoided every snare in the heavily-trapped room.
The glimmering gem on the table is reflected in the cloudbeast's bright eyes.
An anonymous letter, alongside an indistinct photo of the gem. The letter specifies a time and place for you to be, alone.
An incredibly obvious trap. You should not go, Pepe.
Well, I have to go. How else am I supposed to get the gem?
But...
But what?
You agreed to take me to the man-made lake in the city tonight.
Did I say that? Or maybe you misremembered and it's actually tomorrow night?
Ever since my brain was replaced, I have not misremembered a single thing.
If that's true, you would have remembered where all the gems were.
You could ask Anat. She wrote a loooooot of official-looking documents about you lately.
I also have a few observational reports and paragraphs of a draft for a paper on this ancient general, actually.
Maybe she can take you there.
...I might need to take a few more shifts, if so.
Do you really have no idea who sent this letter?
The tourist who got second place seemed like a nice lady.
I might have been too easy to read at the Harvest Gathering. Everyone could probably tell I wanted the gem.
But we need it to get more information out of Zubayr. Whether it's info on my hidden treasure or data for your thesis.
Even so, I advise you not to take the risk.
Even if I've been across all of Sargon by now?
I do recall you nearly failing to make it back twice when we were still in school. Good thing Titi and I decided to look for you both times.
Oh yeah. You recruited a team of adventurers on burdenbeasts to find me, all so I could judge which of the two of you translated some ancient document more accurately.
If Titi was here, I might have some backing on going to the meet-up spot.
Good thing she isn't here.
...She's not? Where is she, then?
'Cause inconvenience'... She said that like I was out to mess things up for her.
*sigh* I helped her out with all the documents before. She wouldn't faint from all the pressure at the office, right?
—! Wh-What was that noise?

If you're my cute little jewel construct, then come out like a good kid. No need to freak me out...
I'm going to count to three. If you're not out by then, I'm going to use my Arts on you. You've been warned.
One, two... three!
Meow—!!!
Oh, darn it! It's you again, little cloudbeast!
Sorry, buddy. Do forgive me. *sigh* Alright, here, some pats on the head, please don't get mad.
I know you're a bit of a bad-tempered scratcher, but let me explain! I've got a good reason for being a bit jittery today!
Meow...
Do you still remember when we were all at the museum earlier? I got freaked out by a bunch of things suddenly jumping all over the place.
...And then, today, I got in another fight with Anat.
Please, please, please don't be as mad at me as her, you cute little cloudbeast you.
(Purring)
*sigh*...
I know, I know, we're always getting into fights—over academic matters, of course.
I know that she really wants to publish her research for everyone to see.
I also know that it's not easy to get the opportunity to hold a special exhibit like this, so she's being real cautious, and also kinda overstressed...
So stressed that in the end, she didn't dare press on with her original goal.
It's obvious I'm trying to make sure she doesn't give up on the special exhibit, right?
Medjehtiqedti unconsciously speeds up her cloudbeast petting.
As she does, Mio purrs louder and louder.
But the girl pays no heed. She is lost in her thoughts, petting and petting and petting and petting...
She used to be real quiet in the past. Whenever she was nervous, her arms and legs would flail about.
Whenever she'd have to give a speech, everyone would be snickering at her by the end of it.
But even if her voice was overpowered by the laughter, she would persevere and finish her speech.
*sigh* If only I could just rub her ears to give her the courage to persevere in other things.
Medjehtiqedti cups the cloudbeast's face in her hands and rubs it vigorously as she vents her feelings, without the slightest bit of interest in why the cloudbeast is purring so loudly.
Suddenly, she feels a warmth in the palm of her hand.
Whoa! Was that a hairball?! Don't spit it on my sleeve!
—Eh?
A jewel?
Maybe I could go to the meet-up spot alone, but with a monitoring device hidden on me. We could get some private security to lie in ambush nearby.
It's far too last-minute for that, Pepe. Security companies won't have the manpower on the eve of the Flood Festival, not to mention you can't negotiate with them in the middle of the night.
Well... How about renting an aircraft to support me if needed?
Such a flying object would be rather suspicious.
I could smash the door in with my hammer, give them a good fright?
That does seem like it could work...
...But would that not be damaging a historical building?
*cough* I guess that would be a debatable too. Though I'm not sure any building is anywhere near as historical as you...
I am rather fond of what the people who came after me have left behind—
Good! Evening! Everyoneeeeeee!
Oh? You're here too, Pepe? Perfect.
Can you all guess what I just ran into on the road earlier?
Good timing, Titi! We've been discussing something really important, and I need your support here—
Quick, help me make Pepe see sense.
If this was an opportunity to excavate an important historical site, would you just give up because you were in a bit of danger?
You of all should know that an excavation requires patience! You're just eager to prove yourself against your brother right now! This is all just...
Wow, you're really not pulling any punches this time around, Anat...
*sigh* Nobody going to take a guess?
Your clothes are covered in fur.
Thank you for indulging me, Zubayr Effendi. Correct, I ran into a little cloudbeast.
In my age, the people believed that cloudbeasts were bringers of good fortune.
It turns out that in this age, I'm a believer too.
It spit out a gemstone full of luck for me.
...A what?!
Let me see, please!
Hold it.
Anat says you agreed to invest in a new exhibition hall in return for her lending you a gem out of the museum collection.
Alright, I hear you, I'll make an investment in your name.
No, Pepe.
I hope you... No, all of you will agree with me on something else.
Pepe, Anat and... Zubayr Effendi.
I think this jewel can grant our wishes.

eps.name: A Job for a Museum Collection

lines.x: Please queue here for tickets... Please queue here for tickets to the 'Encounter with a Resurrected General from the Time of the Shah' special exhibition!
Please queue here for tickets, please queue here for tickets...
Yes, eyes on me, look at me, not the camera.
Please wait just a moment! I need to straighten my bridal gown!
I worked all night to make this, but I guarantee with my three thousand tales about the Shah that it is historically accurate!
Please note flash photography is prohibited in this exhibition, to prevent damaging the cultural artifacts.
Alright! Lets do twenty burst shots!
Hey guys, I'm finally inside the most mysterious room of the Varjavandabad Museum.
Now, I'm going to smoothly record the movements of this heroic general from all angles and uncover the secrets of the resurrection in the City of Radiance.
Dad, its legs look empty inside.
You hear that, Dad? It sounds like it's empty inside when I knock on it!
Mhm, yes, this batch of goods must be delivered to Higashi. It's not a large order, but we can't come off as unreliable...
Sir, please look after your child.
Sorry, hold on, I'm a little busy... Hey! Come back here! Don't run off touching everything!
Please don't make noise in the museum...
Hey, you're the ones who decided to pull off this open exhibition and even let your exhibit wander around! You didn't even limit the number of visitors, so it's no wonder the place is so rowdy!
Acting Curator Anat believes that 'History and knowledge should be made equally available to all', and thus she has not established a tiered ticketing system.

Also, the sixty centimeter-high guardrails around my display are meant to prevent visitors from intruding into my space. If your child crosses the rails, you will be flouting the rules and regulations of this place.
Please cooperate with the staff...
Gah! I missed!
This guide doesn't say which currencies work.
To be fair, the guide doesn't even say if tossing a coin will bring good luck.
Why not try it anyway, just in case?
...Give me a coin. I should aim for its torso, right?
Please... stop... this is an important historical artifact...!
There's too many of them, I can't handle them all...
Understood. I will use my weapon to slice the incoming projectiles apart.
(Lifts hand)
Arrgh—!
Everyone, look!
I'm role-playing a Nightzmoran warrior from a thousand years ago! Ack, I have been defeated! What a grand historical re-enactment!
My weapon did not make any contact with his body.
Oh, General of the Shah! A few words for your defeated foe, perhaps!
Back in my day, the most common phrase one would hear in the army when beheading captured enemy leaders was 'The insects shall devour your entrails'.
Okay, sure, if you could just say that again...
Hey, hey! Don't come over while I'm still lying on the ground! You're gonna cause a stampede!
Oh no... I should call for backup.
Who could've guessed there would be so many people here to see a walking mummy when the Flood Festival is going on?
Hatshepsut Hanimefendi, I hear you've been spending recent days looking for a particular gem lately.
It just so happens my family is in possession of one, so I've brought it here for you. I hope it can alleviate your worries.
Thank you so, so much... please let me see it...
I wonder if I might be fortunate enough to host you at my home for dinner sometime soon?
So sorry, hanimefendi, but I've been crazy busy lately.
Thank you for taking the trouble to bring me the gem, but I'm sorry to say it's not the one I'm looking for.
Oh, truly a pity. I will bother you no more, Hatshepsut Hanimefendi. Until we meet again.
Uh... how many more do we still have outside?
Seventy-three, and they all claim to have the gem you're looking for.
Why are there so many?!
Just tough it out and take a break at noon. Some gemstone merchants from the Grand Bazaar say they've found some clues, so we'll go over then to take a look.
You call that a break...?
Of course. You'll only be seeing gemstones, no people.
Ah...
Anat Hanim, the Padishah's daughter has been sitting here frowning for the past three hours. Are you still interested in the remaining gemstones?
How many do we have left?
More than two hundred...
...Leave them here, we'll finish looking through this crate first.
Very well, I'll take my leave then.
Anat... did someone come by earlier?
They delivered another crate of gemstones.
Anat, something's wrong... why does your head look like a prism...?
The furnishings too... they're sparkling...
Pepe!!!
The museum has finally opened its doors?
Yes, Aspasia Hanim. The special exhibition tickets are in high demand! I heard wind of it last night and wanted to tell you to queue up early, but I couldn't find you.
It's alright, that's not what I'm here to see. It's the collector I'm looking for.
You seem to be in a good mood.
As I thought, language is a bridge to peace, communication serves as a foundation of understanding, and this historic city is also abundant with knowledge.

*sigh* Sargonians have been proud of their glorious history since antiquity. How wonderful it would be if they were willing to sit down for a long conversation, instead of plundering everywhere they go.
That collector understood how persistent we Minoans could be, after my explanation, and I also gained much from his insights into the city...
Right, there is something I want to ask you.
In modern Menat-Ha'mait, are silver crafts more valuable than those made from gold?
Haha, are you joking?
Of course, it does happen from time to time... for example, under pain of death by a lord ameer.
...I see, thank you for the clarification.
May I ask if that companion of mine has returned?
Yes, with two other friends as well.
Two other... friends?
Very well. The more heads to share ideas with, the better. I'd love to find more Kurantas as passionate and frank as she.
Aspasia walks up the hotel stairs and stops before the door.
Narantuya, are you in there?
I bring good news. That collector who won third place has decided to return my goblet to me after listening to my three-hour speech!
Would you like to come with me to the museum to look at both goblets together...?
...I didn't see anyone go in or out of that room with the gemstone last night, Narantuya.
I was hiding on the roof of the opposite building with my eyes peeled the entire time.
...You know how important this kidnapping is. This is the only way to quickly get a big pile of money.
I'm sorry, Narantuya. We'll go look for the Padishah's daughter right away.
It's too late for that.
The caravan I got to be our cover is about to leave the city. Even if we manage to find and snatch her, we won't have a way out.
So...
Let's focus on what to do next.
Both of you should go back to the museum, but avoid contact with the girl. Report in as soon as you find any other way to make money.
I'll go look for the lost gemstone and return it to Aspasia. That's the least I can do...
You don't have to do that.
Ajazi suddenly feels a deadly silence descend upon the room.
She looks at the intruding stranger, shifts her gaze to Narantuya, and finally turns her head to Ajani with eyes pleading for help.
Ajani raises a finger to her lips with her other hand inching towards the sedative at her waist.
Uh, I can explain, Aspasia.
We were just mapping out our plan. Once it's done, I'll return you your gem, or something of equal value. You can trust me on that.
Oh, and, these are my other two subordinates...
What plan?
I heard you planning to bait the Padishah's daughter with that gem, in order to kidnap her.
...You heard everything?
My apologies, I had no intention of eavesdropping on your conversation. I just happened to be standing by the door.
I believe communication lays the foundation of understanding, but I also know you have a large debt to pay and need money urgently.
So, would you like to tell me the whole story?
To be honest, I don't have a clue where the gem is right now. There's nothing I could ask you to do, even if you want to help.
The past is in the past, and our plan now... Well, we basically don't have one.
You know that's not what I'm talking about.
If I tell you why I owe this debt and need the money, will you help me?
I believe we can discuss a more honest and conscientious way to do it.
Heh, I knew you'd say something like that.
Aspasia, mind holding your hand out for me?
What for?
For old times' sake... I just want to...
Ajani and Ajazi exchange looks with each other.
They are certain Narantuya's call to action is the moment when they hear the crisp clacking of her heels on the ground.
——Ask you for a discount on what I owe you.
...Huh?
I've been staying with you for weeks and even took your gem. I have to pay you back for all that.
This coin purse holds one thousand and two hundred dinars. I know it's not enough, but... I mean, we're such good friends, aren't we?
Narantuya...
Ah, there's a bunch of foreign coins in there too. Some merchants might not recognize them, but I'm sure you'll find somewhere to spend them.
Here's some gemstones too. They're tiny and impure, but they make up for it in number.
So... could you be a bit flexible with my debt?
...Narantuya!
Are you so unwilling to have an actual discussion about this with me?
'Communication lays the foundation of understanding', right? I already get you, though.
Maybe you spent day after day in your temple copying statues to work out the basics of swordsmanship, or maybe you followed your parents and took an oath passed down through generations.
More importantly, you're one of the innumerable followers of the Minoan heroes, so I'm sure your soul is pure.
You'd definitely help out someone about to fall from a tall building, and you'd speak up to anyone who used more... exceptional methods.
Yes, you're right.
I believe a scale of good and evil lies within all our hearts, and one exists within the laws of the land as well. People ought to act according to the balance of that scale.
But I understand your point of view. Perhaps you would say the laws set by the lords ameer and padishahs are of abnormal balance, and one should act as one's heart dictates.
However... I dare not trust myself, Narantuya.
If the day does come when I'm caught by the lords ameer and they give me some ridiculous death sentence, will you feel sorry for me?
Of course.
Hah. That's all I need. Looks like you think my death would be a great loss for all Sargon too.
Your help in my grand project would significantly lessen the risk of us failing midway. But I understand if you don't want to.
People come and go. I'd love to have everyone join me and stay by my side, but if I care too much about those who leave, aren't I just as stupid as the ones who resent me?
What I mean is... let's not make it difficult for each other.
...Very well.
I consider your debt settled.
*sigh*...
You're injured.
I'm fine, it's just a scratch...
You should see to it. I've met many a warrior who lost his life after neglecting a minor injury.
Times have changed... I'll tell you all about modern medical science someday.
Are you going to rest?
No... I need to tidy up these soaked notes first...
Soaked?
It's a super long story, and I don't know if I'm sane enough right now to tell you the whole thing.
Before we parted this morning, you promised to share the information you obtained with me. Stay true to your word, Pepe.
...Okay, I found absolutely nothing today.
I made an announcement, and lots of people came to see me with similar-looking gems, but they were all useless stones.
I spent the whole day watching gem after gem fly past my eyes until my head was spinning. Then I went to get a drink and take a break, but the two kids next to me suddenly started fighting.
They knocked over the table and my drink, spilling it onto my notebook, and that's how I got this bruise...
That's all I have to share with you, Zubayr.
Got the information you needed from that?
Do you... wish to hear what I experienced today?
Zubayr...
Many strange people came... though they were rather amusing as well.
Let's pick this back up tomorrow. You don't need to sleep, but I'm exhausted.
Pepe... I do not wish to stay inside the museum.
Just bear with it for a few more days... we need to help Titi with her exhibition until its over. That's how we got the gem from her, after all.
Once the exhibition is over, I'll take you to look for the other gemstones.
I do not want to look for gemstones either...
What's wrong, Zubayr? As the guardian of the Shah's treasury, aren't you supposed to recover all your memories and resume your original duties?
Are you not the one more eager to locate the treasury?
Ah... yeah, you're right. I mean why else did you think I woke you up?
Pepe. I am already awake, and you can no longer treat me as a mere artifact. My thoughts and actions are my own to control.
I do not wish to recall all my memories so soon. At least for the moment.
Tomorrow we will have fine weather, and I wish to take a walk outside, free of all worry.
People are going to freak out if they see a museum exhibit getting some sun all alone on the streets!
I already told you, I am not an exhibiting artifact!
I'm sorry, Zubayr...
It's fine.
It's already so late though, and I'm REALLY tired, so... we can talk tomorrow, okay?
Good night.
In the empty room, Zubayr tries to press the wall switch and turns the light off.
After a moment, he tries again, and the light comes back on.
Meow?
Mio stands in the middle of a night-shrouded street, staring at a window in the distance with a light repeatedly flickering on and off.
And then—
The light stops flickering, and glass shatters.
Once the south wind passes, it'll be the drought season over there.
Will I manage to make enough money before then?
Alright, prepare to jump...
Whoa!
Girls are really something these days... What are you doing? Throwing your life away?
What, isn't this normal for the Flood Festival?
I want to ask the river god some questions.
Not by jumping into the river though...
Also, which history book did you even learn this custom from, girlie?
The ancients did ask the river for answers when they were unsure of something, but later, they realized many problems could be solved with money...
Thanks, but that life hack won't work for me.
I can answer your question, though.
The lamplighter raises his lamp and approaches Narantuya.
Before the southern wind blows through, you won't have enough money to satisfy your own desires, but it will be what you need.
...I'll rephrase my question and be more specific: where should I go to look for the money?
To the depths of the sands.
This is Sargon, that doesn't do much to narrow it down.
I can only answer one more question.
I thought the people of Menat-Ha'mait were generous sorts with unlimited samples to offer?
No... wait, that's not the question I wanted to ask.
I want to know... will that rigid Minoan come looking for me?
Yes.
As soon as the lamplighter finishes, Narantuya catches a glimpse of a shadow on the edge of her vision.
The figure stops in the empty street and looks at her for a moment before turning and leaving.
...Aspasia?
Aspasia!
The shadow crosses the street, and the crimson Kuranta chases after it.
Narantuya can only hear the sound of the wind, the rush of the river, and a song from the lamplighter that gradually fades into silence.

lines.st: ♪May I see Him by the waterside, may I follow Him to eternity♪

lines.x: ...I lost sight of her?
I, Narantuya, actually lost track of a target? Must have been a ghost.
...*sigh*.
Forget it, I'm not stupid.
Everyone here is always praying to see a spirit or two, and I heard no footsteps earlier.
So, this only means I really did see a ghost, yes?
She sits down on the curb.
On the other side of a flowerbed sits someone listening to the river silently in the dark.
The benches by the river tend to get wet, you might want to wipe it dry first.
You're not actually scared of ghosts, are you?
What, did you see that shadow too? Did it spook you? Need me to protect you?
...I'm feeling nostalgic.
I didn't get a good look, but I'm certain it felt like the soul of a late friend or kin.
Two spotting a ghost is always better than just one.
I hear anyone who spots that shadow will receive the blessing of Menat-Ha'mait, but it looks like the two of us here are down on luck.
...I merely got into an argument.
What a coincidence. I'm guessing you didn't win?
I would not be sitting here if I did.
Haha.
You know what? Somebody told me ten minutes ago that the locals are never troubled because money can always solve their problems.
I thought that made sense. What's there to be sad about, if you're not as desitute as me?
Today, someone threw something at me, another soiled the new cape I got last week, and I was inexplicably married twenty times to people of all genders and ages.
No one was interested in listening to me either.
O-Oh... that's pretty unlucky.
How old are you? They stole your identity for a marriage registration?
...What?
Your voice is a little strange, but you sound young. No offense.
*sigh* I think I was pretty lucky, when I was around your age.
One time, we were herding some stolen burdenbeasts back home, and the sky suddenly turned dark. Our headscarves couldn't shield us from the sand flying our way.
But we had plenty of manpower, and quickly found a somewhat different area to dig into.
Guess what we found? Two houses buried in the sand, just enough to fit everyone. Even came with beds and tables.
...Common furnishing you find in ancient tombs.
Hahaha! You know your stuff! I only found out a few years later.
Those were wonderful days.
Indeed... Time can alter how rivers flow, establish and eradicate cities, bleach bones with the sun, elongate and intensify history.
It makes one miss the past, and nostalgia... can be a fearsome thing.
Yeah. Miss the past all you want, but you're never getting it back.
How are the people you've been missing?
They're alive, but more surviving than living.
The worst part is that they can't just move on. You have no idea how many of them sold off their blades.
So as their boss, I owe them.
I mean, I just need to build a new pile of gold, and those stooges will cheer up and return to the way they used to be.
I hope that is the case.
What about you?
I... don't know either.
It's like how people risk their lives to catch a glimpse of the river god, but all they end up seeing is the last face they would ever want to see. Is that not a terrifying prospect?
It's not that scary, if people still choose to chase that mirage.
Well, that's what my mother always told me, back home:
'We Nightzmora must be the masters of fear, and will never be dominated by it.' That's what they all say.
—You're a Nightzmora?
Yeah, why—
Narantuya feels a pat on her shoulder and turns her head on reflex.
She sees no one but the silent street, with its lamps being lit one by one from a distance.
The lamplighter hums a tune as he walks up to them, climbing up a ladder and opening the glass shade to light it.
AAAAAAAAHHHHHHH!!!
Help, HELP! There's a dead guy haunting meee!!!

eps.name: A Job for a Museum Collection

lines.x: What are we waiting for?
A phone call. Might be a novel thing for someone as old as you.
How much longer must we wait here?
No more questions, just wait and see.
Can I take the call? Someone explained how it works to me a while back...
Nuh-uh-uh. No can do. They'll think we're a scam call the moment they hear your robotic voice.
Alright.
Right. As you mentioned earlier, us working together is an imitation of the greatest alliance in history.
Indeed.
Sooo... can I ask what's in store for me, if I help you find your gem?
Oh wait, I did get something already. I got a golden slipper thrown in my face after you scared me last night.
Do you have any suggestions?
——No, no, I wouldn't dare ask for more. Letting me live is already more than enough. You're one of Sargon's forefathers, I should be escorting you on your quest. So please, don't be hasty.
Though, the artifact that won first place at the Harvest Gathering seemed to resemble this very gold slipper, inlaid with sapphires...
Yes, the two of them complete the pair.
So...
Is making a ruckus in the dead of night also a way to make a fortune?
They originally came in a pair, but one was lost forever in the depths of the river, so this other one naturally has its value halved.
Oh.
Please entertain my curiosity. Are all modern Nightzmora... like you?
Beats me.
I've looked into the customs and beliefs of many tribes for my plunders, fences, and abductions, but I never looked into my own birthplace.
I never went back after that one sunrise.
I don't suppose... you are presently on your Khaganquest?
Khaganquest?
(Ancient language) Khaganquest.
——Nuh-uh, that's the last word I ever want to hear.
I'm not doing it, not even if my ancestors compel me to.
You mean you never performed your coming-of-age ritual?!
Yeah? It's not that important.
I might have a few drops of Nightzmoran blood in me, but they didn't turn me into a tyrant or a conqueror, and I have no intention of letting them influence my life either.
No, it is very important.
How will your forebears recognize your blood, if you don't embark on your Khaganquest?
It's fine, most of them pretty much disappeared off the ends of the earth. Unlike someone I know who suddenly sprang to life at the museum just to lecture me.
So yeah, what about you?

I've heard that in the Shah's time, people would shave their heads bald when they came of age, that they applied perfumed ointment to their scalps. But some say the scent smelled more like a stench... so I'm pretty curious if that's true.
I also...
Oh, there we go.
Huh, I didn't expect to get news so soon.
Yes, hanim. It just so happens the gem trading post's overseer sent word that he has a gem to trade, and it fits your description in all aspects.
Good, I'll go to him right away.
But... it will be difficult negotiating a deal, even with my introduction.
What? I heard from my subordinates they could simply sell their gems at the reception.
Selling is easy, but not the other way round. The trading post does not operate like a regular store. You need to offer something heavy enough to balance the scales.
Something heavy? In value?
...I have an idea.
Ahem, yes. This is the deal I wanted to discuss.
The ancient mummy from the recent museum exhibition... The Nameless General of the Shah's Very Court.
The young overseer shoots her a complicated look, and leans down to the huge gilded box.
Zubayr lies inside silently, with his hands folded across his chest.
Narantuya thinks this is honestly Zubayr's time to shine—even his dreadful golden mask seems to be emitting a soft, sacred glow.
This box looks familiar.
It is a sarcophagus taken from the museum in its original condition... a replica of it, I mean.
...(Lightly taps Zubayr's body)
(Examines his chest with a flashlight)
Um, this isn't a regular artifact you can find anywhere. So if you could be more gentle and professional with it...
...I hear it came back to life, and can move on its own.
About that... I'm sure we all know the dead don't come back to life. Just Arts at play is all.
I mean, wouldn't you be afraid, if it really could move?
Afraid it might run off after, I buy it?

Well, uh...
Oh, is that black one your pet? Haha, what a beauty!
Of course the gem trading post's overseer, who controls the flow of money throughout the entire city, would have such a unique pet.
(Whisper) Is that him, Woof?
(Sniffs)
(Sits besides the sarcophagus)
Hey, look. It sniffed out the millennia-old stench you can never fake.
It's as real as my sincerity in making this deal with you.
Of course, I know how precious this artifact is, and it'll be quite troublesome for you to handle it—I'm basically offloading my troubles to you, so I'm not exactly hoping for a windfall.
But I guarantee the museum has no way of tracking me down...
No, this is an extremely heavy offer you've made.
Though it's still within the measuring limits of my scale.
Uh, okay.
What do you want in exchange? I hear you came for a gemstone.
Well, that was the original plan, but I thought of something else on the way over. I don't need that gem anymore.
I'm an outsider, so ingots will do.
You wish to be paid in Pure Gold? I'll go fetch the appropriate scales.
Please bring this inside to keep others from seeing it.
So... we got a deal?
Yes.
Ha, I love a man who does easy business!
I don't like the feeling of sleeping in here one bit. You didn't have to get your subordinates to bring it here in the first place.
Ssh, don't speak... Just hold on a bit longer, I'll come retrieve you soon.
Seize the chance and find the gem you're after before the day ends.
Alright, please do come back. I still need you for something.
This is the price I'm offering. Any objection?
Phew... ♪Remember the crescent moon on the blade, the youth's sincerity washed into gold♪
I'll be back for sure... no objections at all! Nope!
Well, I'll be going now.
By all means.
(Blinks at Zubayr)
(Blinks at Narantuya)
I look forward to any future cooperation!
I never said we'd have any future cooperation.
Hah, me neither—in the near future, at least.
That old thing wants me to run errands for him? Sorry, but see ya!
...She should have understood my signal? Narantuya, if you're truly a descendant of the Nightzmora...
A treasury awaits you.
He finally followed the notices I sent around and came here...
Mio, Woof.
Well done, Lazvar.
You have earned our praise.
It seems that Zubayr broke the window... and left on his own?
I'll go find him and bring him back.
Leave it to the museum, Pepe. You haven't had a proper rest for several days now.
The envoy from the City of Gold is about to reach my father's territory, Anat. I need every second I can get.
Pepe...
You and Titi have gone through a lot tidying up the damage to the museum. I suspect there are a few spots Zubayr might be. I'll go check them out right away.
Do you know why I didn't want to lend you Zubayr at first, Pepe? After I got your letter saying you were coming back, I wanted to tell you...
Two months ago, I was on a business trip in your father's domain. There, I saw that the envoy from the City of Gold had already arrived.
Your brother's already gone to the City.
You knew, but you never told me.
You were so happy in your letter telling me what you found. You even used twenty exclamation marks in a row at one point.
I... I didn't have the heart to tell you.
Then why'd you lend him to me? You got me excited over nothing...
This is a city of jewels, Pepe! There's just so many gems here that I thought you'd never find the ones you were looking for!
I thought you might give up on your own, when the time came.
B-But the envoy was supposed to arrive in August, right? Why were they ahead of schedule?
Your father wrote a letter requesting they come in advance.
So they already decided a long time ago. They just didn't want me to kick up a fuss, so they waited until I was out looking for artifacts of the Shah before sending the kid off to the City of Gold.
But He told me he would wait to see my discovery before making his decision...
The elders always make their own decisions, Pepe. Nothing us can do about it.
Did you see the brat too?
...Yeah.
He must be real happy with himself, getting to go to the City of Gold. I don't know how many times we've fought in public or in private.
I don't think he was particularly happy about it, Pepe. From what I could see, he looked like he's been hiding some sadness this whole time.
He called me to the study that day, saying that it was unlikely the two of you would meet again in your lifetimes, and that he hoped that I could pass a few words on to you.
What did he say?
He said he hopes you will remember his voice. After that, he left.
He didn't have much luggage on him, and only a portion of it was his. Everything else had to do with you and your father.
Pepe...?
Ever since I was little, everyone would tell me how becoming the Shahanshah's historian would continue my family's honorable legacy. But honor is usually bought with sacrifice and devotion, Anat.
Entering the City of Gold and becoming the historian means never leaving ever again, never to see your friends or family. All that comes with you is your scrolls and books.
But... you really hate being chained to a desk, don't you, Pepe?
Yep. I wanted to spend my entire life walking across Sargon, free to look for every remnant and vestige left behind by history.
But I couldn't do all that... because I couldn't let the boy get what he wanted.
Is it so tough for you to let your brother get his way that you'd rather be unhappy yourself?
Ever since we were little, everyone who saw him would praise him as a quiet yet cool-headed boy, perfect historian material.
I was the only one who knew how bad he was, and that he loved fooling around. Making him sit in front of a table for more than a second was torture for him.
Then why did the two of you fight over the position of court historian?
Because he didn't want me to go to the City of Gold.
He didn't want me stuck there by myself.
So you...?
I'm kinda the same...
I don't want him to be stuck there by himself either.
A gem that would bring relief to sore legs after standing for too long? No, I have no need of it.
A gem that would cause the other party to apologize of their own accord? Of some use, but also not what I seek.
I can sense that the gem is extremely close, but there are far, far too many jewels in this house...
Hearing footsteps coming from the corridor, Zubayr immediately hurries back to the huge gilded box to lie down.
He senses an attentive gaze falling upon him, yet not a word breaks the silence. He cautiously opens his eyes a silver—
And sees a sand-colored animal.
Oh... This cannot be...
The creature jumps into the gilded box, bringing its face right up against Zubayr's mask, as if lightly sniffing him.
A sable beast also sticks its head into the box.
Mio... Woof...?
Seems your memory's nearly back to normal.
Mhm. The scent does not lie. You are no fake.
Shut it, Woof.
How did you two become so small...?
Aren't we the way we are now because you failed in your duties as a retainer?
My duties...?
If I still looked as I did back then, I'd be carried out to parade every day. How would I have time to help you find gems, then?
It's better for all of us the way I am now.
Mio, Zubayr has no way of understanding what we're talking about right now.
I already warned you of this before he arrived.
(Angry glare) Hmph...
(Guiltily looks away)
Never mind. He'll soon understand what we experienced these past three hundred years.
Woof, call over our new retainer.
The young overseer walks through a near-endless hallway to reach Zubayr.
A blue fowlbeast suddenly flies atop the wall of the trading post, prodding at a mechanism inlaid within.
Gears mesh together and springs creak, and the fowlbeast plucks a gemstone out of the safe that just opened.
It places the stone on a thick brown piece of velvet on a tray, presenting it to the overseer.
His gaze is calm. His gaze is impassioned.
I am pleased to meet you, Zubayr Effendi... No. Rather, long time no see.
This stone is one of my weights.
In addition to this gem trading post, there are also all the jewels circulating through the Grand Bazaar...
I would like to know, Zubayr Effendi, if all these gems together would outweigh the soul of a sinner?
The door's unlocked, come in.
I said come in, did I not?
Is that you, Narantuya?
There is no-one in the black of night outside, but the rapping on the door continues.
Aspasia looks up and sees a construct of exceedingly ancient make clinging to the top of the door.
The gyrating construct beats on the jewels embedded into the door with all speed, shaking out grains of sand from who-knows-where.
A new form of robbery...?
As Aspasia stretches out her hand to take it down from its perch, it drops the door ornament it was holding onto the ground.
The gem glimmers for a moment in the darkness. Soon after, Aspasia sees the gem and the construct disappear into the entrance to the sewer.
Jewels snatching away a jewel.
Good. Merely gems retrieving their compatriots from the clutches of their excavators.
To history!
To the dead!
To our dear Narantuya!
Even though she's not here!
But she's the one who treated us to all this carob juice!
I never knew a mummy could be that valuable. How about we go check out the museum storehouses tomorrow and see if we can scrounge up another dead guy?
Oh, Ajazi, Ajazi, ye of little thought. Only looking for the dead in the museum? Can't you see the big picture here?
We need to go look for dead people outside the museum too!
Yeah, you're right.
But wouldn't we become officers of the law if we did that?
...Did you lose something, Ajazi? I feel like you're not your usual self.
Eh? No, definitely haven't lost anything here. A great bandit like me wouldn't let anything of mine get stolen.
Now shut up already and keep drinking!
I thank you, young one.
Those metal creations who accompanied me have returned... as has my duty.
You and I have gone into the night, awake and alert. Now, the sun rises once more.
In the stuffy summer heat, a girl sits at her desk, one hand sorting through a thick stack of reference materials while the other wipes sweat from her forehead.
She hears footsteps behind her but doesn't bother to turn around.
There are almonds on the desk, but he already knows.
Why are you still reading, my dear sister?
It's such a fine and sunny afternoon, my dear sister! Let's go play outside!
You don't plan to sit with your nose in those dusty papyrus scrolls your entire life do you, my dear sister?
I talked to Father, my dear sister. He said he would consider making me the Historian of Shar-Agade.
Being a padishah isn't so bad, my dear sister. So just give up already.
Enough! Stop bothering me, you little pest!
She quickly turns her head, but there is nobody behind her...
Only the scroll she tossed at her brother, sprawled out on the floor.
Are you there, Pepe? Quick, let me in!
I'm here, come in.
I've been sending you messages on the communicator. Why didn't you respond?
I turned it off. I've been looking at books and scrolls all night. What happened?
A lot happened last night. Did you seriously not hear a thing holed up in here?
Nope. Didn't hear a peep.

You sealing yourself away to work is really...
Forget it, I don't even know where to start.
Just hear it yourself.
Emergencies across multiple districts of Menat.
Many metallic humanoid constructs have broken into homes and stolen large quantities of gems.
Residents have reported these robberies to the city guard, and the captain assures us that an investigation is currently underway.
Metallic humanoid constructs...?
According to an eyewitness, he saw these metallic constructs breaking out from the museum last night...
What?!
Well... Everything I wanted to say, the radio already said.
Anat says the museum staff followed their tracks and ended up at the Grand Bazaar.
Is this Zubayr's doing?
We suspect so, but nobody's seen him at all lately. You were in contact with him the longest. Could you help us look for him?
Got it. I'll head over right away.
What about you and Anat?
We're in charge of the museum, we have to find all the artifacts.
We're going to head out and have a look ourselves.
If I find anything, I'll let you two know. Be seeing you.
Phew...
Err, hold on, we interrupt this program for some breaking news.
Residents have noticed the water coming out of their taps is muddy and full of silt.
The city guard reports that they have dispatched emergency teams to inspect the city's old underground water pipes. Due to their complexity, this process will take some time...
Just what is going on here?
Someone has already told me the tale of this place.
The prosperity of the Grand Bazaar is borne upon gems, alongside spices, silks, and crafts, all flowing through the river of commerce to bestow riches upon this land.
The gem trading post and the gems with unusual Originium circuits have transformed Menat-Ha'mait into a morning star on the crown of sand...

Several houses begin to turn into sand. People throw open their windows or rush out their doors, staring in disbelief at the ancient buildings they have lived in for generations, and then at Zubayr, who stands in the middle of the street.
But Zubayr pays them no heed.
He stoops down and picks up a piece of jewelry from the pooling sands, its gem glinting within the metallic helix structure under the sun.
The fresco you mentioned is straight ahead, Zubayr Effendi.
My mother's father once told me that the painting depicts the lives of ancient Sargonians and records the birth of Menat-Ha'mait.
'Even if the river dries, and the city is buried beneath the sands, it will not fade.'
...'Will not fade', indeed. Just like every gem that has passed your hand and mine.
But it was neither dried river nor burial by sand that brought calamity to the city of yore.
But a flood that destroyed even the peaks of the grand edifices. What remained of their splendor was eventually buried by the sands, becoming a secret of a thousand years.
Each rise of the sun and fall of the moons is a tiny repeat of the endless cycle.
The brevity of a day and the span of a lifetime...
The birth of a new city, the fall of an empire, and mountains to dust, are but all similar repetitions.
Effendi, if I may. I believe that while the fresco captures only a fleeting moment from centuries past, its value lies in that this moment will be repeated countless times to its beholders.
The Shah ordered this fresco made not to ensure his name would be remembered throughout history.
He hoped to leave behind his interpretations of time and life. To serve as a foundation that the people yet to come could imitate.
Here did the Shah of Past and Future build his trove, and so it was that during the long Aslan Wars, which raged after his death, countless lords ameer coveted this place.
Until the hellish heat waves from the far south brought great change to the climate. A devastating flood ensued, toppling walls, diverting rivers, and leaving the land forever scarred.
And so the people searched the sands for traces of the trove, but found nothing. Thus did they believe it was washed away in the deluge.
The treasure hunters of the lords ameer left, but the artisans and merchants came, establishing a new settlement by the riverside.
I have been watchful of all that transpires here.
I was ordered to guard the trove. That is my duty.
I dwelled beneath the sands, fending off miscreants who dared approach for countless moons.
As for you, young one, you should know better than any other: the Shah's treasure trove lies beneath this city.
'Fourth, the liver. We go into the night, awake and alert, for duty and obligation.' ...I thank you for returning this gem to me. It has restored more of my memories.
The treasure trove started with tens of thousands of skilled craftsmen gathering at the then-desolate Menat-Ha'mait.
From majestic labyrinths made of gold to hand-crafted gems inlaid with Originium circuits, the artificers created them all and buried them beneath the sands.
Later generations mostly believed that after exhausting all their energies on their work, these artisans were all buried alongside their creations.
But in truth, some still lived in a secluded village to the southwest of Menat-Ha'mait, and for many generations, they guarded the secret of the trove's entrance.
It is now your turn to speak, young one.
What follows next involves the story of your family.
The young overseer shuts his eyes tight, as though drowning in the heavy sands of memory.
The tale has been passed down from generation to generation, seared into the mind like the scorching sun above the desert.
Its heat burning, singeing, and blistering.
Three hundred years ago, an army comprised of the forces of many lords ameer and padishahs found that village.
They interrogated the villagers on the location of the trove's entrance, cutting the hands off of anyone who refused to speak.
A gemwright who had already lost his hands could no longer bear to see more of his kin suffer, so he approached the army and proposed to lead the way.
Before the trove, he watched the terrible battle between the army and its guardian unfold. He saw that though the guardian was defeated, the gates would not open without his Arts.
The furious army cut open the guardian's chest, plundering every gem inlaid within and without his body.
Although the gemwright used every technique he knew to guide his young child in restoring the guardian, he never woke up.
After that, a set of scales was set up in the Grand Bazaar of Menat-Ha'mait. Once an adult, the gemwright's child buried the guardian, then established a trading post specializing in gems.
His descendants have been waiting there ever since.
Every generation did all they could to ensure Menat-Ha'mait's gem trade would become even more prosperous.
Whether it takes a hundred years or a thousand, the day will come when the people scour the sands of Sargon, and all the gems will gather in this city once more...
...And I shall return them to you.
The overseer dares not open his eyes as he feels Zubayr's sistrum press upon his chest.
Yet, visions of centuries passing flash before him in an instant.
He witnesses suns rise and moons fall, cities flourish and then crumble, returning to the sands from which they came.
He sees the ancient guardian walking in the boundless desert.
The guardian spreads his arms, basking in the light of the stars, facing eastwards to welcome the sunrise.
Feeling watched, the guardian looks down and meets the young overseer's eyes.
Under his merciful gaze, the overseer trembles.
He trembles in anticipation of the momentous words the guardian is about to utter.
Your family's journey for penitence has been long indeed...
You are all free now.
It has been quite a while, you two.
Many centuries have I forgotten my duty, but now I return to the mortal realm. I, alongside this city, shall make up for the time we have squandered.
But at the same time, I have a request, and I hope the two of you will agree to see it through.
If that Nightzmora truly is the descendent of Kharanduu Khagan, then I will honor the Shah's promise and open the trove for her.
Woof, aid me in ascertaining her lineage.
Leave it to me.
I must put her to the test as well. If she is ultimately incapable of completing her Khaganquest, she will never be considered a true Nightzmora.
No matter what may come, make sure she takes that step.
I understand.
Do I have to go as well? I never thought those Nightzmora were any good.
There are too many drunks among them, spilling their drinks all over the place. I don't feel like having to lick my fur clean again.
No, Mio. I need you to aid me with another matter.
Out with it, young one.
After I sank into slumber, the Shah's treasure trove suffered heavy damage. A dereliction of my duty.
I must admit that even with the constructs at my side, my strength would not be enough to hold back the combined armies of the lords ameer and the padishahs.
Just tell me your decision. I'll get it done for you, like I always do.
I met a girl. I believe she can help me.
Following the footsteps of the Shah, she found one of my lost gems. If not for her, I would not have awakened.
Oh, you mean that kid? I've seen her, seemed like a good egg.
Motivated, lively... too bad she's a little too stubborn.
Reminds me of her.
Indeed. After searching through my memories, the girl's conduct and demeanor feel rather familiar.
She reminds me of my elder sister. When we walked down the streets of the City of Gold, she could tell me the origin of every brick and tile.
She was especially fascinated by the things of yore.
What do you want me to do with the girl, Zubayr?
I need a companion. One who will await the Shah's return alongside me.
Help me confirm if she is worthy, Mio.
If that's your wish, Zubayr.
Then I shall go.
Hey! Watch where you're going!
S-Sorry, effendi! I was in a hurry. Are you okay?
Well, I supposed nothing's broken.
Where are you off to in such a hurry? Don't you know there's trouble?
First those metal constructs sent all the gems in the city to the Great Bazaar, now they're tearing down houses and chasing us out of the bazaar.
The constructs are tearing down houses?

Yeah! There are flying stones and debris everywhere.
Hey, why are you still going that way? Didn't you hear what I said?
I need to see what's going on!
What do you think you can do, girl?
Hey, girl!
...No one's here. They've all gone.
By Horakhet...
The streets, usually bustling with people, are now empty, save for constructs moving in pairs or triplets.
They raise their arms, and houses turn into sand.
From the fallen sand, tall pillars and walls emerge.
Yikes, that almost hit me.
Meow...
Who was that...?
Mrow...
Huh, where did this critter come from?
Meow!
Go, this is no place for you.
Go! Shoo!
The animal is not daunted by her shooing and instead sits down to leisurely lick its own fur.
A suspicious Pepe decides to go around it. As she takes a step, the animal looks up and gazes straight into Pepe's eyes.
In that instant, Pepe feels as though her feet have become nailed to the ground.
But it is such a small animal.
She gathers the courage to take another step forward.
A deafening roar stops her. She feels her eardrums hurt.
She cannot imagine the sound coming from a tiny animal that barely reaches her shins.
She covers her ears and closes her eyes, wincing as the sound wave almost knocks her over.
As her ears stop ringing, she feels a massive shadow descending upon her.
Pepe opens her eyes to see a formidable creature prowling in the middle of the street, faint patterns with a wondrous gleam roving upon its sandy fur.
Transfixed, she meets the beast's gaze and feels as though her very soul is being drawn into those golden eyes.
Her breath and heartbeat seem to fall under its control, ready to cease at its will.
Unable to move a single muscle, she watches helplessly as the beast slowly approaches, its crimson maw parting...
And swallows her whole.
All goes dark.
Anat, Acting Curator of Varjavandabad Museum. Yes, we are aware, but unfortunately we do not have the resources...
Our experts are being deployed across the city. We do not currently have the manpower to assess your losses...
Of course we value your sponsorship, but...
Anat, Acting Curator of Varjavandabad Museum... no, we are unable to offer any comment at this time.

...What? No, the museum is already on lockdown. No collection can leave the building.
The museum has nothing to do with the constructs in the city...
Hello?
*sigh*...
Anat, Acting Curator of Varjavandabad Museum...
*cough* *cough* Sorry, we're getting a lot of sand and dust here.
I assure you, on behalf of Varjavandabad Museum that we will offer restoration services at the earliest opportunity...
*cough* *cough* ...Appraisal?
The constructs have built a statue in your garden? And several pillars? May I have a description?
...Yes, the ornaments you described fit the traits of first-century art style. It had gone into decline prior to the founding of Menat-Ha'mait, and many of its techniques are considered lost.
I will send someone as soon as possible.
Titi, can you go over?
Do you think it's possible? That the constructs can build, sculpt, and master techniques believed to be long lost?
I don't want to, but...
Looks like you have a bad feeling about it too.
Which is why we need to see it to believe it.
What is it, Titi?
You said 'see it to believe it', right?
Carried by the dry winds, sand creeps up like an overflowing river, covering the feet of fleeing passersby.
Grains of sand gather before their eyes and form into shapes, as though poured into invisible molds.
Pillars and walls begin to rise from the streets.
Beast reliefs and patterns imitating Originium circuits, coupled with the worship of sundials and time, representative of the era when Lugalszargus's influence was at its strongest.
Titi, now's not a good time...
This might be the 'memory' of the constructs. Can it even be counted as a form of restored artifact?
But we didn't see any constructs nearby—
—Ruuuuun!!
Where did they come from, anyway?
*pant* *pant* Questions later, Titi! You've got to run faster than me, and pull me along!
We... we're done for if we run at my speed!
Don't worry, you're running fast enough. Hang in there, try not to pass out, and let me observe them a little longer...
Oh, and don't look back. Probably not good for your heart to see them smashing the curator's favorite murals to pieces.
Wh-What?!
The museum that the curator entrusted to me... the padishah's favorite museum... it's all over...
Have mercy on me, Shahanshah! Watch over me, Exalted Lugalszargus!
Watch out—!
Great Horakhet, I pray to you again! Allow your child to see your face again! I-I want to see the sunrise tomorrow! I want to see it shine once more on the museum!
The babbling Anat trips and falls.
Titi catches her just before her face hits the ground, but the two of them hear a loud noise.
It sounds like a machine turning on.
*pant* *pant* Narantuya! We finally found you!
We haven't heard from you since you bought us drinks last night! We were so worried!
I haven't had a shut-eye in days, so I slept in longer. What's there to worry about?
Anyway, good timing. Give me a hand.
Why are you still helping other people carry stuff?
Shh, I'm not doing this for anyone else, it's for myself.
This is obviously an old statue. Better to get something out of it than to just leave it on the streets.
But Narantuya... Isn't it made by one of the metal constructs?
Huh? What metal constructs?
Lay it out for me slowly. I might have slept too soundly last night and missed some headlines.
I thought you were the sharp one, Narantuya. How could you have missed it? There are weird metal constructs all over the street. Didn't they come at you?
They're tearing houses apart, making walls and pillars out of sand... and grabbing gems. Maybe you should check?
Everyone lost something last night, from onyx necklaces on their necks to astrolabes that simulate the moons and stars.
Is that what happened to your hairpin?
Wha... where did my hairpin go?
You got one thing right, I'm sharper than you.
And I'm starting to feel that something is off.
Anyway, now that I have the intel, time to come up with a plan.
Take off any gems you're wearing and hide them. If those metal constructs are robbers, let them come for me first.
Join the fleeing crowds and get your smoke bombs ready.
But where do we go?
Everyone else can hide in their homes or at least stay in hotels behind locked doors. Are... we going back to the sewers?
No problem, I know the way. I'll help us find our sleeping bags.
No... we're going back to the museum.
The museum? How could that place be safe when they'll hire people like me and Ajazi?
Think about it. Who are the experts on caravan routes, desert terrain, dune movement, burdenbeast habits, and tribal faiths? Bandits, meaning us.
So, who are the experts on the dead coming back to life, ancient curses, and necromancy? The museum folks.
...Makes sense.
I hear something.
Perfect timing. I want to see what those metal constructs are all about.
—Alright I get it now. Let's go!
Are we practicing our escape?
No, this IS the escape!
Let's hope smoke bombs work on these things!
She turns around, raises her hand, and prepares to pull the pin on the smoke bomb—
Suddenly, the streets grow quiet.
The sound of marching machines disappears.
All the metal constructs Narantuya can see slow down.
They stop chasing them and gather around Narantuya.
Calm and docile, they watch like tamed burdenbeasts as Narantuya walks past them.
—And back.
What the hey...?
Am I dreaming?
It's not a dream, Narantuya. I pinched myself just now. It hurt.
Thanks. Then I won't try it myself.
What's going on?
Say... I saw a shadow of the river god the night before.
Maybe it really did bless me.
I understand now.
Ajazi, Ajani, go on to the museum without me.
What about you?
Divine duty. I need to find someone.
The river god must have wanted me to act the hero, and show Aspasia that I'm the boss.
'Those who stand by me need no longer be enemies of your warriors.'
The jewel constructs are following the pact of the two human sovereigns a thousand years ago. They recognize her blood...
You were right, Zubayr. She is the descendant of Kharanduu Khagan.
Come, young Nightzmora. Walk into the dream—

lines.st: You should all bless me. Bless me with a dance under the morning sun and the tapping of your scabbards.
—Oh, right, I forgot you sold your swords. Then beat the pots and pans. Play the anvil like a drum with your pens.
They say the roads of Menat-Ha'mait are paved with jewels. Well, I'm heading for that road now.
I know most of you will tell me to stop being delusional. But why can't I make it rich?
Why can't I buy us the best life?
I have to try.

lines.x: Where am I—?
—Oh, my departure ceremony! I must have danced myself dizzy.
I give you my blessing, Narantuya.
There's a caravan I know that regularly visits the City of Radiance. If you do make it there, seek them out.
Hah, I knew you'd have some useful connections!
One more friend, one less trouble. Thanks for the blessing.
I'm still worried, Narantuya.
About what? How many times have I told you? No one's going to get revenge on you when I'm gone.
This has nothing to do with you. You chose to work for me and not Asim because I'm just that charming.
Old Asim resents me for it and speaks ill of me everywhere he goes, but he's just being petty.
And those jealous haters had to band together and set me up... my fault I'm so lucky that I attract both gold and people.
But you're the one I'm worried about, Narantuya.
if you just wander into the desert with an empty satchel, what will you do when you can't find water or beasts?

Huh? I know we somehow ended up poorer than we started, but I'm sure I at least have some coins for jerkies and preserved fruits.
Why would you think that? Has anyone been starving without telling me? Who is it?
I'll give them some coins right now—
Narantuya reaches into the purse at her waist.
As her eyes slowly widen in disbelief, she unties the beast hide pouch, turns it inside out, and begins shaking it.
A few grains of sand fall onto the endless desert.
Strange. I don't remember bumping into a beggar or vagrant...
Actually, I vaguely remember crossing paths with a few rich men?
Narantuya, I thought it over, and I think I should teach you my secret to finding water in the desert.
I don't have to worry about becoming a sacrificial tribute to a bandit chief anymore, so there's no need to keep this a secret any longer.
—Good. I like this blessing.
The sands can enjoy those lost coins then.
I'm worried too, Narantuya.
Hmm? And what are you worried about? That I'll abduct another child slave from a caravan who will replace you as most illiterate?
I'm worried about the two black blades of yours. You haven't used or maintained them for a long time.
It's precisely because maintenance is a chore that I avoid using these antiques. I much prefer modern materials and mechanical forging—
—Wait, where are my blades? And my dagger? And my supply of paralyzing toxins and spore casks?
*sigh* I'm worried about your tendency to pick up new recruits, Narantuya.
You'll be setting out alone. It's great if you could get some useful crew. But most of the time, it's people like me and Ajazi that you have to save...
'Setting out alone'? You girls aren't coming with me to the City of Radiance?
...which is why Ajazi and I worked for two months in a logging camp and threw all the coins we saved into a river they say flows into the City of Radiance.
I hope you will find them when the river floods, and that your Khaganquest goes smoothly...
Okay, I appreciate this blessing too, even though the rainforest is at the downstream of the river.
...Wait.
Did you say Khaganquest?
Yes, your Khaganquest.
You're all set for it, right?
No? When did that happen?
...Come to think of it, I am indeed 'all by myself with nothing but the weapons from my parents' right now...
...Meh, pass on the city. I'm out!
The young Nightzmora turns and strides over to her burdenbeast pack.
At that same moment, towering walls rise up from the sand. Even part of the piercing sunlight seems to be blocked off.
Where are my burdenbeasts? And my tent?
Narantuya rubs her eyes.
As if trapped in a nightmare of her own making, her field of vision narrows until only one path remains.

lines.st: 'Step forth, prove your bravery, embark on your Khaganquest.'
'Just take the step—'

lines.x: No way! This Khaganquest is too scary! I'll die!
Help——!!!
Strange. The metal constructs here are not moving.
Someone's on the ground? Hello, can you hear me? Hold on, I'm on my way—
——Out of my way!
What did you say?
I was just lying on the ground and a giant beckbeast had its paw on my head?
I think it's a beckbeast, but I failed to catch the creature before it vanished into the sand and dust.
It's no wonder my head hurt while I was dreaming. There was a voice I heard making 'oowoo...' sounds. My face is fine though, right?
...Yes.
Phew.
How did you chase the beckbeast away? I spent so long yelling at it in my dream with no luck.
The situation was urgent, so I resorted to force.
Wow, why didn't I think of that? Sometimes violence is the solution!
I knew you'd be a reliable subordinate. Thanks a lot—
Wait no, you're not a subordinate.
...We'd best be leaving. These constructs could start moving at any time. It's not safe here.
Seems pretty safe to me.
*sigh* The river god presented me with a chance to prove my heroism, and I ended up falling asleep on the street...?
The dream was nice though, at least the first half of it.
...The streets are in chaos, while you lay on the sands... and had a good dream?
Yup. I dreamed about my departure ceremony. Everyone was cheering me on. Even their beat was spot on.
How nice would it be, if my former subordinates were actually that supportive when I was setting out for Menat-Ha'mait!
I talked to Ajazi and Ajani briefly, after you left the hotel.
Did they tell you most people think I'm delusional?
They did.
But consider this: if one has to eventually wake from one's dream, why not indulge in a bit of fantasy before that happens?
(Can't help but laugh)
Perhaps there remains much of each other that we do not know.
But for now, I'm simply glad to see you safe.
Aspasia extends her hand to Narantuya.
So, does that mean we're all good—
—Hold on.
What's wrong, Ajani?
Narantuya! Help!
The museum! Isn't safe! At all!

lines.st: 'Horakhet has witnessed.'

lines.x: —Who's there?
Titi, did you say something? I heard something...
I didn't.
...But I heard it too.
Uh, no way...
Can these artifacts talk?
